question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on whether EURneffy nasal spray, used for the emergency treatment of severe allergic reactions, will be made available in Northern Ireland.
To ask the Minister of Health for an update on continuing healthcare.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail their understanding of the impact of financial pressures on the ability to expand student numbers at Magee.
To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any support he can provide to ensure there is sufficient provision of affordable swimming lessons in South Belfast.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department holds any data on the availability of swimming lessons in South Belfast.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she has had with Ulster University, in relation to the staff redundancy scheme.
answer_text: I, along with my officials, continue to engage with all Higher Education Institutions regarding the potential impact of the Department’s challenging draft budget.  	This will be a worrying time for staff and I am working to secure a funding settlement that can deliver a sustainable Further and Higher Education sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any consideration she has given to introducing a maximum safe temperature for workplaces.

To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 36801/22-27, for an update on when the regulations relating to the Domestic Abuse (Safe Leave) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 will be introduced to the Assembly.
To ask the Minister of Education whether a Small and Micro Business Impact Test has been undertaken in relation to the draft Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.
answer_text: A Small and Micro Business Impact Test (SAMBIT) sits within the Northern Ireland Regulatory Impact Assessment (RIA) requirements. RIA screening was undertaken on the draft Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) Strategy prior to its issue for public consultation on the 14 December 2026.The screening acknowledged that the draft ELC strategy and proposed actions set out within it have the potential to impact on businesses, both directly and indirectly. It further acknowledged the potential to impact on the voluntary and community sector recognising its role in ELC provision. A full impact assessment on the complete draft strategy was determined not to be required at this time. The regulatory impact, including that pertaining to small and micro businesses, of each action will be further and fully considered once the final strategy and associated actions have been agreed and during the implementation design stage.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any consideration she has given to updating health and safety legislation.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any surveys that were carried out prior to the decision not move forward with the pedestrianisation of York Street, Belfast.
answer_text: My officials carried out a number of on-site observations with regard to pedestrian movements and vehicle volumes at York Street, Belfast.  These observations confirmed that the current pedestrian crossing and overhead walkway between the university buildings were sufficient at this location.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the development of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee Scheme.

To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 30536/22-27 and AQW 41624/22-27, to detail (i) the rational as to why there has been a delay in bringing forward a public consultation; and (ii) when it will be published.
To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 41349/22-27; (ii) AQW 41166/22-27; and (iii) AQW 41169/22-27.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of three-bedroom properties (i) currently under construction; and (ii) due to begin construction in the 2026-2027 financial year, in the Greater Belfast area, broken down by constituency.

To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) whether his Department has made any assessment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in schools: guidelines and guardrails, published by the Scottish Government on 25 March 2026; and (ii) whether he has plans to introduce similar guidance for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department is focused on the guidance needed for schools here and has been leading a rigorous process, with a range of educational partners, to develop guidance in respect of the use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) in which draws on research and practice in other jurisdictions across the United Kingdom and internationally.  Northern Ireland is in a unique position as the Education Authority’s Education Information Solution (EdIS) provides a safe online platform for use by teachers and pupils which ensures that data inside a school’s own ecosystem is not shared publicly.  The work being taken forward includes the development of a wide range of online support materials which will be made available on its EdIS GenAI Platform.  A comparison with the guidance produced in Scotland highlights that there are many similarities in terms of the advice it provides to education professionals and the checks and balances required to ensure GenAI is used safely and responsibly in schools.  I hope to issue guidance to schools shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any engagement (i) he has had; (ii) or intends to have, with the Scottish Government in relation to their Artificial Intelligence in schools: guidelines and guardrails' document, published on 25 March 2026.
answer_text: My Department is focused on the guidance needed for schools here and has been leading a rigorous process, with a range of educational partners, to develop guidance in respect of the use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) in which draws on research and practice in other jurisdictions across the United Kingdom and internationally.  Northern Ireland is in a unique position as the Education Authority’s Education Information Solution (EdIS) provides a safe online platform for use by teachers and pupils which ensures that data inside a school’s own ecosystem is not shared publicly.  The work being taken forward includes the development of a wide range of online support materials which will be made available on its EdIS GenAI Platform.  A comparison with the guidance produced in Scotland highlights that there are many similarities in terms of the advice it provides to education professionals and the checks and balances required to ensure GenAI is used safely and responsibly in schools.  I hope to issue guidance to schools shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the Radiology Department at the Belfast City Hospital is making use of the My Care system.
To ask the Minister of Education to provide an update on funding for TinyLife.
answer_text: I would refer the member to my previous answer to AQW 41254/22-27 published in the weekly answer booklet for week ending 27 February 2026. The position has not changed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any assessment has been made of the salary of a community pharmacist.
answer_text: The Department has no role in determining or assessing the salaries of community pharmacists in Northern Ireland.Community Pharmacies are privately owned businesses and while they operate under a contractual framework with the Health and Social Care (HSC) to provide services and dispense medicines, community pharmacy owners, pharmacists and other support staff working for them are not direct Health and Social Care (HSC) employees.  Community pharmacist salaries in Northern Ireland are determined by individual private employers.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when the AI Strategy will be published.
answer_text: We will shortly launch a consultation on a draft Artificial Intelligence Strategy. Following the conclusion of the consultation, a final Strategy will be brought to the Executive for consideration prior to publication.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had in relation to the future of the Country to Country music festival in Belfast.
answer_text: I have not had any discussions about the Country to Country music festival in Belfast.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the development of multi-disciplinary teams to deliver improved breast cancer services.

To ask the Minister of Health to detail how much additional funding will be made available in annually recurrent funding to support breast cancer services, beyond the £5 million non-recurrent funding allocated in 2025/26.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how her Department communicates with residents living on unadopted roads, when unsafe lamp posts require removal.
answer_text: When street lighting columns are identified as being unsafe or deemed to be at high risk of failure, my Department will act in the interest of public safety and remove or make safe as a matter of urgency, without prior communication to public and residents. This work is reactive, therefore, my Department’s street lighting contractors will, where possible, liaise with residents to ensure minimal disruption whilst the work is ongoing.Where columns are identified as end of life and meet the requirements for a replacement scheme residents will be given advance notice by letter of the planned works prior to the work commencing.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in relation to breast cancer services funding, to detail (i) how much of the £5 million allocated will be spent before the end of the 2025/26 financial year; and (ii) whether any underspend will be rolled over into the 2026/27 financial year.
To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the roll-out of smart electricity meters.
answer_text: The final Design Plan is being finalised. It will set out the roles and responsibilities of the key organisations involved in delivering the roll out, alongside detailed arrangements for consumer protection and engagement. It will also outline the foundational and technical requirements, as well as the overall roll out strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) any discussions she has had with the Minister of Education in relation to his proposed reforms to GCSEs and A Levels; and (ii) any assessment that has been made on how this will impact (a) higher education; and (b) further education provision.
answer_text: I discussed these matters with the Education Minister at a Cross-Departmental Ministerial Meeting in November 2025, and officials from my Department continue to engage with Department of Education counterparts through established 14–19 governance structures.In respect of higher education, the potential implications of the proposed reforms on crossborder mobility have been highlighted. The review of A-level grade equivalencies recently implemented in the South means that, currently, northern applicants can now achieve top CAO points with three A-levels and one AS-level, making it easier for students from here to study at institutions in the South.  Changes in respect of AS Levels may impact the progress made on reducing barriers to north/south student mobility. In respect further education, my Department is currently considering the potential impact of the proposed reforms on future further education provision and will continue to engage with the Department of Education as this work progresses.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, given the significant impact that our school system has on skills development, to detail any engagement she has had with businesses on the forthcoming changes to the Northern Ireland curriculum, being brought forward by the Minister of Education.
answer_text: I have not had direct engagement with businesses specifically on the forthcoming changes to the curriculum being brought forward by the Education Minister.Officials in my Department are continuing to work closely with counterparts in the Department of Education through established 14–19 structures to understand the implications for skills development, including the interface with businesses as this work progresses.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how digital inclusion is being considered as part of the work of the Office for Digital and AI.
answer_text: Digital inclusion is a core consideration of the draft AI Strategy, which is explicit in identifying training and literacy as one of its eight core principles. The aim is to ensure that digital transformation delivers benefits across society, supported by strong governance, transparency and accessibility.The Strategy is intended to guide departments in prioritising inclusion throughout service design and delivery. Making services easy to use and accessible is a shared responsibility. The Office will continue to advocate this approach as AI solutions are developed to ensure they are applied ethically, generate social value, and align with human needs and expectations and are supported by strong, meaningful human oversight.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme will continue beyond April 2026.
answer_text: The continuation of the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme into 2026/27 was announced on 11 March: Education Minister announces continuation of Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme | Department of Education

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for an update on her Department's work to develop an Ethical Investment Framework; and (ii) to detail when it will be published.
answer_text: Work on the Ethical Investment Framework remains in development with its publication planned for within the current mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the chickenpox vaccine, including when parents can expect to be able to avail of it.
answer_text: A combined measles, mumps, rubella and varicella (chickenpox) vaccine was introduced into the childhood vaccination schedule from 1 January 2026.  In line with advice from the Joint Committee on Vaccinations and Immunisations, the MMRV vaccine replaced the MMR vaccine and is now being offered to children at 12 months and 18 months of age.  There will also be a selective catch-up programme for MMRV, for children under the age of 6 years old, without a history of chickenpox. This is due to commence from November 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail a timeline of all engagements the Education Authority have had with Rosetta Primary School, in relation to the need for urgent repairs to their school hall, within the last two years.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has provided the following timeline in relation to the need for urgent repairs to the assembly hall at Rosetta Primary School:MonthDetailFebruary 2024Senior maintenance staff met with the school principal to address urgent repairs needed for the Gym Hall roof. Following this discussion, Maintenance Service decided to close the hall due to health and safety concerns. March 2024 – May 2024A structural engineer was commissioned to conduct a comprehensive survey of the roof’s integrity, which included specialised damp investigations. Based on the engineer’s recommendations, safeguarding measures were implemented to support the roof structure and contingency plans were established with the school authority to allow temporary use of the nearby church hall.June 2024 – September 2024A specialist engineer was assigned to develop a detailed specification for the roof upgrade. Given the complexity of the design and the need for local authority approvals, the project was transitioned to EA Minor Capital Delivery for execution under their minor works programme.January 2025An external architect-led design team was appointedMay 2025Initial structural report completed, which identified other adjacent areas of poor condition. A more extensive condition report was initiated , as the scope of proposed works was extended.January 2026Extensive condition survey carried out.March 2026The EA received the formal condition report from the design team on 4 March and this will now be incorporated into a feasibility report, to inform the business case. It is anticipated that the feasibility report will be completed this month.A draft programme for delivery for the Roof Upgrade Works including an additional Toilet Provision, has been prepared by the appointed architects, but the actual delivery programme will be dependent upon the extent of works being undertaken and will be confirmed within the approved business case.The EA’s Chief Operating Officer and the Assistant Director of Infrastructure and Capital Development met the school and they reaffirmed the commitment to replace/repair the schools assembly hall. It was also agreed that the EA’s Maintenance Service will visit the school in the coming weeks to review the condition of the school and identify any maintenance priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail when works to enable Rosetta Primary School to reopen their school hall will take place.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) appointed an external architect-led design team in January 2025 to review the works required to the roof of the assembly hall at Rosetta Primary School. Following an initial structural survey, the scope of the proposed works was extended and a more extensive condition survey was initiated. The EA received the formal condition report from the design team on 4 March 2026 and this will now be incorporated into a feasibility report, to inform the business case. It is anticipated that the feasibility report will be completed during March 2026. A timeline for the work will be determined when the business case is completed and the preferred option identified. The EA will liaise closely with the school on a timeline for completion of the work.While the roof works are being planned and delivered, the EA has established a licence agreement with the neighbouring church to enable the school to use the church hall, with EA covering this cost. The EA has also installed a gate in the boundary wall to enable pupils to access the church grounds directly from the school site.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she is having with (i) the UK Government; (ii) the Irish Government; and (iii) international partners, in relation to the rising cost of oil as a result of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 30536/22-27, to provide an update on the review of funding provided by his Department to support students on pre-registration healthcare programmes.
answer_text: My Department is continuing to review funding arrangements for commissioned and non-commissioned tertiary level courses leading to HSC careers.Officials are engaging with key stakeholders to develop options for a public consultation on that issue. This collaborative work will continue until the review is finalised. I would then hope to launch a public consultation on future options in this area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any next steps she plans to take, following the recent Credit Union (Modernisation and Reform) public consultation.
answer_text: Officials are scheduled to brief the Economy Committee on the outcome of the Credit Union (Modernisation and Reform) public consultation on 22 April, following which the next steps will be finalised and published alongside the consultation analysis.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) whether any assessment has been made of the rising cost of home heating oil, as a result of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East; and (ii) any action she can take to ensure consumers are protected from significantly increased costs.
answer_text: I have been closely monitoring the scale and pace of price increases, which have been significant, and I remain in close contact with counterparts in the British and Irish governments to understand the broader market impacts. This monitoring will continue as global events evolve.In response, I held a round table discussion with fuel suppliers and retailers on 5 March to emphasise their responsibility to act fairly and transparently, and my officials are working with our agencies to ensure there is no unjustified increase in costs for consumers.Given that more than 60% of households here rely on home heating oil, I have also pressed the British Government to consider appropriate forms of support, similar to what was provided following the Russian invasion of Ukraine, to protect households and businesses from further financial pressure.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance the detail the amount paid by (i) the Northern Ireland Civil Service; and (ii) other public bodies, as a result of the Apprenticeship Levy, in each of the last three financial years.
answer_text: The information provided is for the NICS departments and those public bodies that are participants of the (NICS) HRConnect payroll system.2022/20232023/20242024/2025NICS Departments£3,512,788£3,619,977£4,093,876AFBI, NIAO & NIO £109,266£113,795£132,858

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the development of the Victims and Witnesses of Crime Strategy 2026-2032.

To ask the Minister of Health (i) whether all assessed mobility equipment needs in care homes are being met in full; (ii) to detail how compliance with (a) the Nursing Homes Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005; and (b) the Regional Nursing Home Minimum Care Standards 2015, is monitored in respect of mobility equipment provision.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23750/22-27, for an update on when the (i) footpaths; and (ii) road, at Onslow Gardens, Belfast, will be resurfaced.
answer_text: Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2026/27 financial year will be published in my Department’s annual report to Council.My officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any consideration he has given to introducing Benedict's Law in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer to AQW 41034/22-27 published in the Weekly Answer Booklet for week ending 6th March 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on any actions taken by her Department in relation to the Targeted Business Support for Childcare Providers Market Research, since publication in September 2025.
answer_text: The Childcare Providers Targeted Business Support Research Report was published on the Department’s website on 24 September 2025 and concluded that the sector would benefit from additional business advice.  This recommendation is being taken forward by my Department, who are actively engaging with stakeholders to develop a specification and identify providers for this service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any process that nursing and residential care homes should follow in order to request mobility equipment.
To ask the Minister of Health for an update on how he is addressing unregistered childminding.
answer_text: HSC Trust Early Years Teams investigate all reports of unregistered childcare, serving enforcement notices under the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 where appropriate.  In addition, my department continues to promote the benefits of using registered childcare and raise awareness of the risks associated with unregistered childcare, through the www.familysupportni.gov.uk website.The developing Executive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy will also assist in tackling the issue of unregistered childminders, by promoting the value of registered childminding, as well as making childcare more affordable and accessible for parents who need it.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in relation to mobility equipment, to detail (i) the total number of requests; and (ii) the average wait time between initial assessment and provision, to nursing and residential care homes, in each of the last three financial years, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.

To ask the Minister of Education to detail any immediate financial support he can put in place for childcare providers to ensure they can (i) remain open; and (ii) recruit and retain high-quality staff.
answer_text: As part of the £80m budget agreed by the Executive in the last two financial years for the Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) Strategy, I allocated funding to the Department for the Economy (DfE) to lead on a scoping exercise to develop a clearer picture of the business support requirements of the childcare sector.  The published report in September 2025 concluded that, while there was insufficient evidence to justify government financial support for the sector as a whole, there was a need for specialised childcare business advice.  A copy of the DfE report can be found here. As set out in the draft ELC Strategy, currently issued for public consultation, the establishment of this service is prioritised within the category 2 actions to be taken forward from April 2026.In addition, to support recruitment and retention to the sector, in this financial year I have allocated funding to: the Department for Communities to recruit childminders to the sector through Labour Market Partnerships; additional funding to the Department of Health to recruit more Approved Home Childcarers and enhanced training for the sector as a whole; to the DfE for the development of on-line accredited courses to develop flexible learning opportunities for the sector as well as a mapping exercise to identify gaps in management qualifications.Further proposals to support the workforce, including the recruitment and retention of staff are set out under Objective 3.3: Enhance the Early Years Infrastructure through a Skilled and Valued Workforce and include the identified need for a robust and ambitious ELC workforce plan.				The scale and pace of delivery of the final strategy, once agreed by the Executive, will be determined by the resources made available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any data sharing agreements that exist between his Department and the Victims Payment Board, to ensure information provided for the purposes of Personal Independence Payment applications does not have to be provided again for the purposes of applying for the  Troubles Permanent Disablement Payment Scheme.
answer_text: My Department has a Data Sharing Agreement (DSA) with the Victims’ Payments Board in relation to Industrial Injuries Disablement Benefit (IIDB) claims. This DSA does not include the information provided during a Personal Independence Payment application.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when her departmental response to the consultation on low carbon hydrogen will be published.
answer_text: Departmental officials are currently considering responses to the consultation. The Departmental response will be published in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when her departmental response to the consultation on the Northern Ireland Postgraduate Tuition Fee Loan will be published.
answer_text: My Department will issue the response to the consultation on the Postgraduate Tuition Fee Loan in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on how Great British Energy's funding announcements will support projects in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Officials from my Department have engaged regularly with the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) and with representatives from Great British Energy (GBE), throughout the development of their Local Power Plan. While GBE operates independently, with its own governance and strategic direction, officials maintain regular engagement to ensure that we maximise the benefits of the Local Power Plan and GBE’s wider strategic priorities.I accepted an offer of £1.66 million from GBE last year to support renewable energy and battery storage projects across our Further Education colleges. This investment will enable them to generate clean, more affordable energy and reduce their reliance on fossil fuels.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they will write to the UK Prime Minister in relation to the issues being raised by Women Against State Pension Inequality compensation.
To ask the Minister of Health how the £5 million allocated to support and expand breast services across Health and Social Care Trusts has been used to improve patient outcomes.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the membership of the Childcare and Youth Work Sectoral Partnership.
answer_text: The Childcare and Youth Work Sectoral Partnership is chaired by Francis Loye, Director of Giggles Early Years.  Southern Regional College carry out the secretariat function, supporting the chair and members. Membership of the Sectoral Partnership includes a range of employers, representatives from sector bodies such as Playboard, Early Years, Health and Social Care Trusts, Department of Health, Northern Ireland Social Care Council, CCEA Regulation and training providers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she has had any discussions with the Student Loans Company, in relation to reducing the interest rates on student loan repayments, within the last 12 months.
answer_text: The Student Loans Company has no role in setting the interest rates applied to student loans. Borrowers from here are treated as “Plan 1” borrowers, this plan type also has borrowers from England, Scotland and Wales, the terms and conditions of this plan type are therefore not a devolved matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to the publication of the Motor Insurance Taskforce Final Report and Actions by the UK Government, to outline whether she has given any consideration to developing a Northern Ireland focused report on motor insurance.
answer_text: The Motor Insurance Taskforce established by the British Government has already fully considered the issues specific to the North. These matters were raised directly through Ministerial engagement, including a dedicated trilateral meeting involving myself, the then British Government Minister for the Future of Roads, Lilian Greenwood MP, and the Minister of Finance, during which the particular challenges faced here were discussed in detail.My Department, together with the Department of Finance also provided formal written input to the Taskforce, ensuring that the North’s unique circumstances were incorporated into its analysis and final recommendations. This included correspondence issued following Ministerial meetings outlining local insurance issues and the factors influencing premiums here. As a result, the final Motor Insurance Taskforce Report already reflects the key issues affecting the North.My Department will continue to work closely with the British Government and key stakeholders on all motor insurance related matters specific to the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration she has given to introducing (i) speed humps; (ii) or any other intervention, to reduce speeding along Queensfort Park South, South Belfast.
answer_text: Regrettably when assessed against competing priorities, the provision of traffic calming measures at Queensfort Park South did not score highly enough for inclusion in a works programme.  The scheme will be kept on the list should further funding become availableDetails of the schemes being taken forwarded are included in my Department’s annual report to council.Concerns with regard to driving behaviours, including speeding, should be reported to the PSNI on their 101 number.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of times the Childcare and Youth Work Sectoral Partnership has met, since it's formation.
answer_text: The Childcare and Youth Work Sectoral Partnership has met sixteen times.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 36987/22-27, for an update on when the Bill of Amendment to the Health and Social Care (Control of Data Processing) Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 will be introduced into the Assembly.
answer_text: I intend to introduce the Bill to the Assembly by April 2026.  The timetable for introduction will remain subject to the NI Assembly’s legislative schedule and processes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 37285/22-27, for an update on the membership of her Department's Artificial Intelligence Advisory Panel.
answer_text: I will be Chairing the first meeting of my Artificial Intelligence Advisory Panel in early March, at which point membership will be made public.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 37378/22-27, whether the 200 metre indoor athletics track available for use for major events is still operational.
answer_text: The operation and availability of the indoor athletics track at the SSE Arena are the responsibility of the Odyssey Trust.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the current status of the cladding of Ravenhill Reach.
answer_text: Confirmation was received from the managing agent that Ravenhill Reach does not have non-aluminium composite material or other cladding that may need to be removed on grounds of safety. For this reason, no application to the Northern Ireland Cladding Safety Scheme will be forthcoming.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when they will publish the AI strategy.
answer_text: The Office of AI & Digital is leading the development of an ethical and forwardlooking cross-government AI Strategy that has been underpinned by extensive consultation.  A dedicated Task and Finish Group, bringing together stakeholders from government, industry, and universities, laid the foundations by examining the key pillars of Data, Governance, Infrastructure, Skills, Public Sector Transformation and Citizens, this group helped shape a shared vision for responsible AI adoption in the public sector.  An external reference panel was also convened to provide independent challenge and expert feedback throughout strategy development, ensuring the approach is robust, ethical, and aligned with best practice.  Following a thorough review of the initial draft AI Strategy by industry partners and NI Departments in December 2025, work has been carried out to formulate a revised iteration of the Strategy which is now subject to Executive approvals and a public consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will consider developing policy regarding medical fitness to drive, during the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: I remain firmly committed to ensuring that our framework for assessing medical fitness to drive is robust, evidence-based and capable of supporting wider road safety objectives. As such, my Department is continuing to review current policy in this area to determine how best to strengthen existing arrangements and identify where legislative change may be required. In my response to your recent correspondence on this issue (COR-0010-2026 and AQW 35705/ 22-27 refer), I had set out how the work is being progressed through two strands. The first focuses on improving and standardising the use of current administrative processes, including those associated with the Source of Danger Notification, and this is the priority in the short term and within the 22-27 Assembly Mandate. The second strand involves more detailed policy analysis to assess potential options for future reform. My officials continue to review the position on fitness to drive in other jurisdictions, including Britain and Ireland, and it remains evident that this issue, along with the challenges arising from an ageing population, is relevant to all and not unique to us. I have recently asked my officials to bring forward legislative amendments to the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 to permit the use of continuous glucose monitoring systems for Group 2 drivers who have diabetes treated with insulin. This will significantly decrease the risk of hypoglycaemia whilst driving, making the roads safer for all. We are also closely monitoring the British Department for Transport’s recently launched consultation on proposed eyesight testing changes for older drivers. The outcomes of this consultation will provide useful evidence to help inform the development of policy options here, including whether new legislative powers may be necessary.While I appreciate that progress may not be as rapid as we would prefer, it is essential that any changes rest on a clear and robust policy foundation. This approach will ensure that the system is fit for purpose and supports the overall aim of improving road safety.I want to reassure you that road safety remains of paramount importance to me. I will continue to focus Departmental resources on interventions that will have the greatest impact on saving lives.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether there is a shortage of wheelchairs in nursing homes.
answer_text: The Department of Health has not been notified of a shortage of wheelchairs specifically across nursing homes across Northern Ireland. The Nursing Homes Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 and the Regional Nursing Home Minimum Care Standards 2015 place explicit responsibility on providers to ensure residents have timely access to suitable and safe mobility equipment. This obligation is supported through statutory regulation, the regional care home contract, community equipment guidelines, and minimum care home standards, all of which collectively require providers to ensure that residents’ assessed needs are met in full.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the Regional Obesity Management Service.
answer_text: Following my announcement on the establishment of a Regional Obesity Management Service for Northern Ireland, my officials are working to develop an implementation plan.  The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) Technology Appraisal (TA) TA1026 - Tirzepatide for managing overweight and obesity, has been endorsed, following review, applicable in Northern Ireland, as an option for managing overweight and obesity.  This has been considered, in addition to reviewing Weight management services delivered in other nations.As part of commissioned service delivery within the Regional Obesity Management Service, adult patients will have access to weight loss medication, if clinically appropriate, as well as lifestyle support to ensure maximum effectiveness.  Further phases will be developed, subject to funding. The service will provide equitable, sustainable and affordable access to weight loss medication to optimise outcomes for those with the highest clinical need. My Department acknowledges that full implementation of this service will require consideration of financial and service requirements, including clinical capacity, specialist support and the development of a coordinated and sustainable service model and care pathways.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any (i) current initiatives; and (ii) future plans, to support the arts, given the substantial economic return generated by investment in this sector.
answer_text: My Department is developing a new 10-year policy for the arts, through the Heritage, Culture and Creativity Programme.  It will seek to develop the evidence and support the arguments for sustaining the work of the arts sector.The Arts Council, as the funding and development body for the arts in Northern Ireland, details its funding initiatives in line with its strategic plan on its website:  https://artscouncil-ni.org/funding/ and  https://artscouncil-ni.org/what-we-do/our-strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any preparatory work being undertaken by her Department regarding Translink's rail expansion plans, including the reopening of the Knockmore Line.
answer_text: In 2025, Translink concluded work to undertake the initial development of four projects outlined as a priority in the short-medium term in the All-Island Strategic Rail Review. These were the reopening of the Antrim to Knockmore line, the Portadown to Derry line, the Portadown to Armagh line and Electrification from Belfast to the Border. High level outcomes from the study were published in December 2025 as part of the Shaping the Future document https://www.translink.co.uk/about-translink/media/pressreleases/shaping-the-future  I have allocated £1m in this financial year to progress priority tasks, including detailed technical work on the proposed connection to Belfast International Airport, engagement with planning authorities, development of signalling and electrification proposals, and assessment of user worked crossings along the route.The delivery timeline for Translink's rail expansion plans including the Knockmore line will be subject to the availability of funding, approval of the necessary business cases, and securing statutory consents.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had with the First Minister and deputy First Minister regarding the establishment of an indoor running track on the Maze Long Kesh site.
answer_text: I have not had any discussions with the First Minister and deputy First Minister regarding this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of (i) day care and after-school providers registered in South Belfast; and (ii) these which enable parents to avail of the Childcare Subsidy Scheme.
answer_text: According to Family Support Northern Ireland (FSNI), there are currently 35 daycare providers and 27 out-of-school providers registered in South Belfast. Of these, 22 daycare providers and 22 out-of-school providers are registered to the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme (NICSS). FSNI and NICSS figures are fluid and subject to change.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the progression of the Seabird Conservation Strategy and Action Plan, in relation to addressing biosecurity challenges for declining seabird populations.
answer_text: My officials have been updating the Seabird Conservation Strategy and Action Plan to reflect responses from the public consultation, and I hope to publish this in the spring, after which, focus will be on the co-delivery of actions with key stakeholders.  I am pleased to note the recent allocation of funding to the PEACEPLUS CMAP (Coastal Management and Adaptation Planning) project, which includes a dedicated work package for biosecurity measures targeting seabird colonies. This initiative will concentrate on Rathlin and Ballycastle, building upon the progress of the LIFE Raft project.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) any work being undertaken in relation to anti-bullying legislation, and policies, in the rapidly evolving context of online safety and AI; and (ii) how this is being communicated to schools.
answer_text: My Department is committed to tackling all forms of pupil bullying type behaviour and recognises the particular risk posed by cyberbullying. Cyberbullying is a particularly complex legal area, drawing on several pieces of criminal justice legislation. It is, therefore, not an issue on which the Department can act or legislate independently. Recognising that cyberbullying and online safety are crosscutting issues, my officials are working closely with the Department of Health, other government departments and key stakeholders to deliver the objectives set out in the cross-departmental Online Safety Strategy for Northern Ireland and Action Plan.While the Addressing Bullying in Schools Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 (the Act) recognises cyber-bullying as one possible form of bullying type behaviour that can take, it does not contain provisions to specifically tackle cyberbullying. However, the Act is sufficiently broad to encompass cyberbullying. In terms of the wider context around online safety and Artificial Intelligence, implementing a robust and effective mobile phone policy in schools can also significantly reduce the opportunities for inappropriate behaviour linked to personal mobile phones. With this in mind, in September 2024, the Department issued ‘Guidance for Schools on Pupils’ Personal Use of Mobile Phones and other Similar Devices During the School Day’, which guidance recommends that pupils do not use their mobile phones during the school day and that phones should not in normal circumstances be brought into primary schools. The guidance sets out a range of potential approaches to restricting the use of mobile phones in schools and key practical issues that should be considered and are likely to form part of a school’s policy approach on mobile phone use.  The Department also funds the Safer Schools NI platform that is made freely available to all education settings, pupils and parents. The platform provides advice, guidance and information on a range of safeguarding issues, with particular attention paid to risks in the online world, emerging social media platforms and online harms. Furthermore, my Department engages closely with the Education Authority (EA) across all of its policy areas, including matters relating to cyberbullying and online safety. This ongoing collaboration ensures that both the Department and the EA are aligned in their approach and able to provide schools with timely, accurate and practical information. As issues emerge or guidance is updated, these are communicated directly with schools to support effective implementation and safeguard the wellbeing of pupils.Finally, I would note that, while I am aware of the risks associated with the use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI), as well as of social media and associated applications, it is important to acknowledge that GenAI has the potential to transform the delivery of the curriculum.  It can be utilised to free up time for school principals and teachers

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following her commitment to prioritising the expansion of Ulster University's Magee campus, to detail any measures her Department will introduce to encourage flexible and part-time study, including distance learning, given their importance for workforce upskilling and economic growth in the North West and across Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I remain committed to expanding Ulster University’s Magee campus, in line the Programme for Government, to deliver greater regional balance and drive economic growth in the North West. While any decisions on modes of study are for Ulster University themselves, an extensive suite of flexible study pathways, including part-time, online, and distance-learning courses across undergraduate, postgraduate, and professional development levels are already currently offered. These flexible routes are designed to widen participation, support lifelong learning, and enable learners to balance study with employment and caring commitments.As part of the Magee expansion specifically, my Department has been working with Ulster University to grow its portfolio of short courses, microcredentials and modular provision. This enhanced offer is intended to create flexible pathways for learners to upskill and reskill at a pace that suits them, while importantly encouraging greater footfall and engagement at the Magee campus. In addition, through the wideningparticipation work being taken forward with the Magee Expansion Taskforce – including targeted outreach, more inclusive pathways, and initiatives to attract and retain learners – we aim to ensure that those who can benefit most from flexible provision are able to access it.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the mental health services that are available in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust for patients with complex trauma.
answer_text: My Department has a formal link with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) under which NICE Clinical Guidelines are reviewed locally for their legal and policy applicability here.  Where found to be applicable, they are endorsed for implementation within Health and Social Care (HSC) organisations. DoH Circular HSC (SQSD) 13/22 sets out process for endorsement, implementation, monitoring and assurance of Clinical Guidelines in Northern Ireland.  As there is no dedicated funding source to support the implementation of NICE Clinical Guidelines in Northern Ireland, any resources required are considered against other competing priorities. Nevertheless, HSC organisations are expected to implement guidance as far as possible within existing resources.NICE have published a range of guidance relevant to mental health. There are a significant number of recommendations contained within each NICE guideline and the extent of implementation varies across Trusts.(ii) There is no formal review of the provision of trauma informed mental health services currently. However, The Department of Health (DoH) and The Executive Office (TEO) are due to commence an evaluation of Phase 1 of the Regional Trauma Network (RTN). This evaluation will assess service effectiveness, governance, collaboration across sectors, workforce development, and outcomes for victims and survivors.(iii) The Belfast Trust Mental Health Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) service offers Trauma focused CBT for patients experiencing complex trauma. Therapists work with individuals to reduce symptoms of PostTraumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) and related difficulties by helping people process the traumatic event, change unhelpful thinking patterns, and regain a sense of safety and control.The Regional Trauma Network offer the following therapeutic interventions to those experiencing trauma as a consequence of ‘The Troubles/NI Conflict: Trauma Focused CBT (TF-CBT)Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing therapy (EMDR) Trauma Focused Counselling Compassion Focused Therapy (CFT) Somatic Experiencing Therapy In conjunction with these therapeutic approaches, the service also offers Occupational Therapy, Physiotherapy and Outreach support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) how his Department ensures that mental health services comply with National Institute for Health and Care Excellence guidance; and (ii) whether there are any plans to review the provision of trauma-informed mental health services.
answer_text: My Department has a formal link with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) under which NICE Clinical Guidelines are reviewed locally for their legal and policy applicability here.  Where found to be applicable, they are endorsed for implementation within Health and Social Care (HSC) organisations. DoH Circular HSC (SQSD) 13/22 sets out process for endorsement, implementation, monitoring and assurance of Clinical Guidelines in Northern Ireland.  As there is no dedicated funding source to support the implementation of NICE Clinical Guidelines in Northern Ireland, any resources required are considered against other competing priorities. Nevertheless, HSC organisations are expected to implement guidance as far as possible within existing resources.NICE have published a range of guidance relevant to mental health. There are a significant number of recommendations contained within each NICE guideline and the extent of implementation varies across Trusts.(ii) There is no formal review of the provision of trauma informed mental health services currently. However, The Department of Health (DoH) and The Executive Office (TEO) are due to commence an evaluation of Phase 1 of the Regional Trauma Network (RTN). This evaluation will assess service effectiveness, governance, collaboration across sectors, workforce development, and outcomes for victims and survivors.(iii) The Belfast Trust Mental Health Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) service offers Trauma focused CBT for patients experiencing complex trauma. Therapists work with individuals to reduce symptoms of PostTraumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) and related difficulties by helping people process the traumatic event, change unhelpful thinking patterns, and regain a sense of safety and control.The Regional Trauma Network offer the following therapeutic interventions to those experiencing trauma as a consequence of ‘The Troubles/NI Conflict: Trauma Focused CBT (TF-CBT)Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing therapy (EMDR) Trauma Focused Counselling Compassion Focused Therapy (CFT) Somatic Experiencing Therapy In conjunction with these therapeutic approaches, the service also offers Occupational Therapy, Physiotherapy and Outreach support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how her Department will ensure flexible study pathways and financial parity for part-time students balancing work, family, and study responsibilities, to drive inclusive and fair economic growth.
answer_text: Engagement is continuing on the development of draft Terms of Reference for a wider Review of Higher Education Funding.It is proposed that the review will include consideration of the financial support available to students, including whether current arrangements remain appropriate and whether support should be extended beyond its existing scope. This will encompass examination of how financial support can better enable and enhance a range of study modes, including part-time and more flexible pathways. The overall timeframe for the review will be determined once the Terms of Reference are finalised and subject to the availability of resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department plans to extend the maintenance and childcare support, currently only available to full-time students, to eligible part-time learners.
answer_text: Engagement is continuing on the development of draft Terms of Reference for a wider Review of Higher Education Funding.It is proposed that the review will include consideration of the financial support available to students, including whether current arrangements remain appropriate and whether support should be extended beyond its existing scope. The overall timeframe for the review will be determined once the Terms of Reference are finalised and subject to the availability of resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail how his Department engages with other Executive departments in relation to managing allergies.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 33980/22-27, for her assessment of the Motor Insurance Taskforce final report and actions, recently published by the UK Government.
answer_text: I welcome the Motor Insurance Taskforce’s report, which sets out important measures to strengthen regulatory oversight and transparency, including the Financial Conduct Authority’s analysis of costs for specific customer groups and an investigation into premium finance. Other actions include enhanced efforts to tackle fraud and uninsured driving through new offences, the development of a good practice code by the Association of British Insurers, and further investment in the vehicle repair sector. These are positive steps with direct relevance to the North.The report also acknowledges the unique circumstances here. As per Action Point 4.4 of the report, my officials have commenced exploratory discussions with key stakeholders involved in the administration and running of the Continuous Insurance Enforcement (CIE) scheme in Britain to explore the potential benefits and feasibility of introducing a CIE scheme here. Once further information is available my officials will present me with findings and options as to next steps for my consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the development of an aviation strategy.
answer_text: My Department has prepared a draft aviation policy consultation following engagement with the aviation sector, local government, and tourism and investment partners. 		The draft has been shared with the Tourism Partnership Board, including the three local airports and key tourism stakeholders. My officials will meet Board members to finalise the draft before presenting it to me. I will then share the consultation document with the Economy Committee, enabling public consultation to begin shortly after.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to provide an update on the review being undertaken by his Department, of guidance on Supporting pupils with Medication Needs.
answer_text: To date, work has been undertaken in collaboration with the Education Authority, Department of Health, Public Health Agency, parents and schools to update the “Supporting pupils with Medication Needs Guidance.  Due to other key priorities taking precedence this work remains in progress.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any consideration he has given to establishing a cross-departmental group on allergy management.
To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on discussions with the UK Government in relation to the Local Growth Fund.
answer_text: There has been extensive engagement between my Department and the Westminster Government in relation to the Local Growth Fund.In October, I met the Secretary of State, Hilary Benn, and Steve Reed, Secretary of State for the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government. We agreed to an intensive period of work to develop a way forward on the Local Growth Fund. This has led to the identification of suitable options for the Local Growth Fund’s 2025/26 £3.8 million Capital allocation, which were agreed by the Executive for submission to Westminster. The Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government will deliver the Resource element of the Local Growth Fund in 2026/27, the practicalities of which are being urgently developed. It is intended that the Executive will manage all elements of the Fund from April 2027 and collaborative working between Departments, local partners and Whitehall will be needed to facilitate this. The £9.3 million made available for Economic Inactivity in 2026/27 is significantly less than in previous years. Westminster decisions on a Capital-heavy funding profile and ongoing delays will be devastating for the Community and Voluntary sector.We need a funding split that meets our local needs and, at least, reflects the more favourable Resource profile given to English Mayoral Authorities. Any support beyond the Local Growth Fund will be a matter for relevant Ministers. No Departments have bid for funding to enhance the Local Growth Fund in the Draft Budget 26-29/30. However, Members will be aware that our Budget position is highly constrained.I have, and will continue to, press British Ministers for an outcome that better meets local needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any additional investment his Department can provide to support the (i) maintenance; and (ii) development of, the Mary Peters Athletic Track in South Belfast.
answer_text: The Mary Peters facility is owned by Belfast City Council and operated under agreement by Athletics NI. Responsibility for the upkeep of the track and all ancillary provisions rests with both the Council and Athletics NI. 		I can advise that no formal proposals or business cases for additional investment have been received by either my Department or Sport NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to the development of an indoor athletics track.
answer_text: AnswerI recognise the importance of Athletics in Northern Ireland and the value of providing appropriate facilities for athletes. In July 2025 I met with Dame Mary Peters and representatives from Athletes NI and the potential options for an indoor track in Northern Ireland were discussed. Following this meeting I asked my officials to engage with Sport NI and Belfast City Council to explore potential options, including whether a Financial Transactions Capital loan could be used to progress plans for an indoor track. This work is ongoing and engagement with key stakeholders will continue as part of the process.There is a 200m indoor athletics track that can be installed at the SSE Arenda for major events. While this is not a permanent facility and therefore not available for everyday public use or regular training, it does provide an opportunity to host high-profile competitions when required.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment of the value of the work undertaken by the Northern Ireland Bureau in China.
answer_text: The work of the Northern Ireland Bureau China supports all the Executive’s Ministers and Departments and promotes a consistent approach across government to maximise the opportunities of our international relationships and by promoting Northern Ireland as a great place to live, visit, study and invest in. The Bureau works closely with overseas embassies and partners across government, to ensure policy and operational development considers our trade circumstances, capabilities, and international market opportunities, to deliver on the Programme for Government objective to grow a globally competitive and sustainable economy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail a comprehensive summary of the work undertaken by the Northern Ireland Bureau in China, within the last 12 months.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of staff working for the Northern Ireland Bureau in China.
answer_text: There is one civil servant working in the Northern Ireland Bureau China, supported by two country based staff.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the membership of her Department's Artificial Intelligence Advisory Panel.
answer_text: My AI Advisory Panel will bring together expertise from industry, academia and government to inform my department’s approach to AI and related digital technologies. Membership of the Panel will be announced when finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Education Authority has given any consideration to making use of Artificial Intelligence to assist with home to school transport planning.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that it is currently exploring new technologies to improve and enhance route planning for school transport and communication with parents. Its transport service may introduce the use of generative Artificial Intelligence technology to assist with its back-office tasks related to route planning activities and to improve productivity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the AI Pulse: Public Sentiment in Northern Ireland on Artificial Intelligence report, published by the Ulster University Strategic Policy Unit.
answer_text: I note the recent publication of Ulster University Strategic Policy Unit’s AI Pulse Report, which provides valuable insight into public attitudes towards Artificial Intelligence. The findings indicate strong awareness and a willingness to engage with AI, while also highlighting concerns around confidence, capability, trust, and ethics. This evidence will inform my Department’s ongoing work to promote responsible AI adoption and strengthen the AI skills pipeline.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rationale behind using images of robots on half of the total pages within her Department's AI Strategic Direction document.
answer_text: The Strategic Direction document has been refreshed in light of this concern.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rationale behind her Department publishing the AI Strategic Direction document, in advance of the publication of the forthcoming Executive's Artificial Intelligence Strategy.
answer_text: The Executive’s AI Strategy is being developed by Professor Helen McCarthy, Chief Science and Technology Officer, and is focussed on the deployment of AI and related technologies to transform the delivery of public services, for the benefit of citizens across the North.  My Department’s AI Strategic Direction framework is related but separate, with a broader focus on the application and diffusion of AI technologies for economic growth in line with my economic vision. My officials are working closely with Professor McCarthy’s team to ensure complementarity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rationale behind her Department publishing the AI Strategic Direction document on 8 December 2025, without an accompanying press release.
answer_text: My department’s AI Strategic Direction is a ‘direction of travel’ document that gives agency to DfE business areas, partner organisations, and stakeholders, to develop a response to the recommendations in the Matrix ‘AI and the Future of Work’ Report. It has been shared widely and is available on the department’s website.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration her Department has given to formally adopting Notting Hill, South Belfast.
answer_text: Road adoption by the Department is governed by The Private Streets (NI) Order 1980 (The Order) and roads, including those within private developments such as Notting Hill, will only be considered for adoption if brought to standard through the Private Streets Determination process or by owners/frontagers in accordance with Article 9 of the Order.The Department can adopt existing streets which have not previously been determined for adoption under Article 9 of the Private Streets (NI) Order 1980 provided it meets the following criteria:The Department is of the opinion that the street should become a public road and a majority of the owners or occupiers of the land in or adjoining the street consent thereto in writing; or A majority of such owners or occupiers request in writing that the street should become a public road and the Department is of the opinion that the request be granted; and (in either case)The Department is satisfied that the street is in all respects fit and proper to become a public road.Should the majority of owners/occupiers wish their street to be adopted, they should first write to the Department.  The Department will then decide whether the street should become a public road or not.  If it does, the onus will then lie with the applicants to fund and complete all works necessary to bring the street up the appropriate standard in order to allow adoption to proceed.Notting Hill does not have a road bond in place, and the Department would therefore not be in a position to contribute towards the works required to bring the road up to an adoptable standard. In this instance all costs would be the responsibility of the residents and property owners.Further information on the adoption process is available on the Department’s website, via the link here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the work to deliver a path at St Ita's Primary School, South Belfast.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively with key partners to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. We are acutely aware that children and young people are amongst the most vulnerable groups using our roads, particularly around schools.My Department recently completed a pilot scheme on Alderwood Hill aimed at improving pedestrian access between St Ita’s Primary School, Cairnshill Park and Ride, and neighbouring residential areas. The scheme included the installation of pencil bollards to prevent inconsiderate parking on footways, and a zebra crossing to enhance safe access to the school for pupils who lived nearby.This pilot scheme has been designed to integrate with a future Lisburn & Castlereagh City Council proposal to deliver an offline footpath between Cairnshill Park & Ride and Alderwood Hill as part of their Carryduff Greenway scheme. As a pilot, it will also help to inform future policy development in this area of road safety and active travel.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she will publish her Ethical Investment Framework.
answer_text: This work is in development and will be implemented within the current mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the development of an Ethical Investment Framework.
answer_text: The development of the Ethical Investment Framework is ongoing. As the work progresses, there will be a structured opportunity for stakeholders and interested parties to shape the Framework. Delivery is planned within the current mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on any discussions her Department has had with Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council, in relation to the proposed Belfast to Carryduff Greenway.
answer_text: Delivery of greenways are generally council led and in providing support, my officials regularly engage with all councils on their greenway priorities. My Department can provide up to 50% of the capital funding required to support delivery of approved greenway projects, and officials will continue to engage with Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council to support delivery of their active travel priorities as plans advance through the various stages of scheme development.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 27403/22-27, for an update on when the Bill of Amendment (i) to address the deficiencies identified; and (ii) to implement the Health and Social Care (Control of Data Processing) Act (Northern Ireland) 2016, will be brought forward.
answer_text: The Health and Social Care (Control of Data Processing) Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 Act received Royal Ascent on 11th April 2016. Following unavoidable interruptions, planning resumed in 2022 for the introduction of the required regulations to enable the sharing of identifiable health data for secondary usages, beyond the limited and specific conditions available at present. The process to draft regulations included a full review of the Act (2016) with guidance from the Department Solicitors office. This review identified a substantive issue in the wording of the 2016 Act, regarding the scope of applicable data which could have encroached on the original intent of the legislation.My Department is currently progressing work to bring a "Bill of Amendment" to address the deficiencies identified. I am now seeking to introduce the Bill in the early new year (2026), to ensure timely advancement and allow for the necessary legislative scrutiny. The timetable for introduction will remain subject to the NI Assembly’s legislative schedule and processes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) an update on the implementation of the Domestic Abuse (Safe Leave) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, including (i) when she intends to publish the findings of the consultation; and (ii) when the regulations will be introduced to the Assembly.
answer_text: I plan to have the necessary legislation in place before the end of the current Assembly mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any engagement he has had with the UK Government in relation to the allocation of additional regeneration funding to towns and cities in Northern Ireland, since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: I met with Minister Alex Norris MP and Minister Fleur Anderson MPin September 2024 in Belfast when we discussed regeneration funding programmes for towns and cities in Northern Ireland such as: the Long Term Plan for Towns, now known as the Pride in Place Programme; Levelling Up Fund; and City and Growth Deals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she has had with the UK Government in relation to the allocation of additional regeneration funding to towns and cities in Northern Ireland, since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: Regeneration of cities, towns and villages is a policy responsibility of the Department for Communities.  The Department of Finance leads on engagement with British Government on the allocation of funding and my officials regularly engage with Department of Finance on funding opportunities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any engagement he has had with (i) providers; (ii) and parents, in relation to accessible childcare for those with special educational needs or disabilities, as part of the development of the Early Learning and Childcare strategy.
answer_text: I have engaged with parents and providers in relation to accessible childcare for children with Special Education Needs or Disabilities (SEN/D) throughout the development of the Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) Strategy to hear directly from them and what they feel needs to be done. I have attended meetings of the ELC Stakeholder Engagement Forum, which includes providers, parents and sector representative groups as well as numerous visits to early learning and childcare settings to discuss the issues and challenges they face in supporting children with SEN/D.  In addition, I held a targeted parental engagement session that included parents who have children with additional needs. I commissioned the Northern Ireland Statistics and Research Agency (NISRA) to undertake a major household survey of parents’ childcare requirements in relation to affordability, accessibility and quality which has provided valuable insights into family and sector challenges, including those added challenges for families with children with SEN/D. My officials continually engaged and held bilateral discussions with other relevant government departments through the ELC Task and Finish Group, and sector organisations as part of the development process. This extensive engagement ensures the draft ELC Strategy for public consultation reflects the needs of families, providers, and children, including those requiring additional support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 35407/22-27, whether she has had any engagement with the PSNI in relation to the inclusion of a marker for medical fitness to drive.
answer_text: There has been no engagement with the PSNI in relation to including a marker around medical fitness to drive in the relevant dataset.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions she has had with the UK Government on the development of an Artificial Intelligence goods adviser and regulatory hub, for Northern Ireland businesses.
answer_text: I note the Chancellor of the Exchequer’s announcement of a £16.6m package over three years to support businesses navigate the Windsor Framework. The need for the British Government to provide more and better support and guidance for businesses has been widely recognised, and the package is welcome in that context. The specifics of the funding have not been confirmed at this stage, beyond the Chancellor’s headline announcement as part of Budget 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the rationale as to why DfI Roads has not responded to the Forge Integrated Primary School Planning Application within the statutory timeframe, despite three years of pre-application discussion engagement.
answer_text: My officials engaged fully with the pre-application discussions for the planning application submitted by the Department for Education for the development of a new primary school building for Forge Integrated Primary School.  The full planning application was lodged in 2025, and the Department’s pre-application advice in relation to access, movement and parking was not reflected within this submission. My officials are currently undertaking a robust analysis of the documentation submitted in support of full planning application for this site and will respond to the planning authorities consultation in the coming weeks.  The Department are supportive of the development of the site, however, road safety is a high priority for the Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. We are acutely aware that children and young people are amongst the most vulnerable groups using our roads, particularly around schools and this must be integral to our assessment and response.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on when DfI Roads will respond to the Forge Integrated Primary School planning application.
answer_text: My officials engaged fully with the pre-application discussions for the planning application submitted by the Department for Education for the development of a new primary school building for Forge Integrated Primary School.  The full planning application was lodged in 2025, and the Department’s pre-application advice in relation to access, movement and parking was not reflected within this submission. My officials are currently undertaking a robust analysis of the documentation submitted in support of full planning application for this site and will respond to the planning authorities consultation in the coming weeks.  The Department are supportive of the development of the site, however, road safety is a high priority for the Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. We are acutely aware that children and young people are amongst the most vulnerable groups using our roads, particularly around schools and this must be integral to our assessment and response.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, given the economic and social importance of medical professionals, to detail any discussions she has had with (i) the Student Loans Company; and (ii) the Minister of Health, in relation to providing (a) tuition fee loans; and or (b) maintenance support, for students enrolled on medical degree programmes, who have already completed an undergraduate degree.
answer_text: The Department of Health has policy responsibility for determining the higher education student support available for medical students studying here. My Department holds the legislation specifying the delivery of tuition fee loans and maintenance products and facilitates and enacts, through legislation and operationally, the Department of Health’s policy position. The introduction of tuition fee loans to support Graduate Entry Medicine courses here followed on from agreement between my predecessor and the Minister of Health, and was delivered through normal channels of engagement with the Student Loans Company (SLC) and the Education Authority (EA).
