Currently my officials are working with the developers and their consultants to ensure the necessary improvements are designed to the required standards and the appropriate licences are in place to facilitate the work.The scheme, when completed, will see the road widened to provide an extra running lane on all four approaches to the roundabout, enhanced pedestrian facilities, new footways and road signage.As this is a developer led scheme, time scales for delivery are outside of the control of my Department, however, it is anticipated that these works will commence in the summer of 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any (i) mandatory; and (ii) voluntary registration processes in place for businesses seeking to operate within the security sector.
answer_text: The registration of businesses within the security sector (Security Industry Authority) is a reserved matter and the responsibility of the Home Office.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to detail any engagement it has had with the Office of the Chief Scientific and Technology Advisor in relation to its Artificial Intelligence working group.
answer_text: As the specific focus of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Working Group is on the use of AI in the Assembly Commission’s work to support the Assembly and the AI Working Group is developing its terms of reference, the Assembly Commission has not contacted the Office of the Chief Scientific and Technology Advisor in relation to the AI Working Group.While the Assembly Commission is mindful of its independence from the Executive and the Northern Ireland Civil Service, it will of course take account of wider government guidance in relation to AI as the work of the AI Working Group proceeds.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to detail the membership of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) working group that has been established to advise the Assembly Commission of AI opportunities.
answer_text: The Assembly Commission has established a working group to consider potential opportunities for innovation that Artificial Intelligence (AI) may offer as it recognises that the rapid growth of artificial intelligence is a development that cannot be ignored by any modern organisation.The focus of the AI Working Group is on the use of AI in the Assembly Commission’s work to support the Assembly.  The group therefore includes officials from a wide range of business areas across the Assembly Commission. The membership of the Assembly Commission’s AI Working Group is listed below:	Head of the Research and Information Service (Chair)	Director of Legal, Governance and Research Services	Director of Parliamentary Services	Head of Legal Services	Clerk Assistant	Head of Facilities Management	Head of Human Resources	Editor of Debates	Head of Information Technology	Head of Communications	Senior Research Officer	Assembly Clerk	Research Officer

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) any engagement they have had with the UK Government in relation to the AI Accelerator Programme; and (ii) whether any representatives from the Northern Ireland Executive will be involved in this programme.
answer_text: An introductory meeting took place on 2nd May 2025 between representatives of The Executive Office, Department of Finance (NI) and the UK Government in relation to the AI Accelerator Programme. The focus of the meeting was on information sharing and to outline the steps required to include NICS staff in the second cohort of the AI Accelerator Programme, which will launch in September 2025.   No representatives of the Northern Ireland Executive are involved in the first cohort of the AI Accelerator Programme which launched on 28 March 2025.   The recently established AI Task-and-Finish Group will consider the issue of AI and digital skills across the NICS workforce as part of the scoping work to establish the Office of AI and Digital. Members of the Task-and-Finish Group will liaise with other administrations to determine useful programmes and interventions for possible adoption as part of the Office of AI and Digital’s future work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the progress of the Energy Strategy Mid-Term Review.
answer_text: The Energy Strategy Mid-Term Review commenced in January 2025 and will complete within the year. The Review considers progress from the Strategy’s launch in December 2021 up to the present and will establish the key milestones to deliver in the period to 2030.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work underway to digitise Northern Ireland official publications which are held by (i) PRONI; and (ii) Queen's University Belfast.
answer_text: There is no work underway to digitise Northern Ireland official publications held by PRONI. My Department has no remit for digitisation of Northern Ireland official publications in the custody of Queen’s University Belfast.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) how the works taking place to improve the Purdysburn Road, South Belfast, will lead to improved safety for pedestrians, including school children and elderly people who regularly walk in this area; and (ii) whether the improvements will include any changes to speed limits along this stretch of road.
answer_text: My Department is aware of the needs of pedestrians, particularly the young, elderly and those with disabilities and tries to provide facilities where a need has been identified. As part of the resurfacing scheme on Purdysburn Road three existing pedestrian islands will be upgraded and a new pedestrian island will be provided between Bests Hill and Greenwood Glen. These measures will enhance the pedestrian infrastructure and promote active travel in the area. Officials have carried out a speed limit assessment on the road in line with my Department’s policy. This assessment has concluded that the existing speed limit on Purdysburn Road is appropriate. The existing road markings will however be reconfigured during the resurfacing scheme to encourage drivers to lower their speed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the Circular Economy Strategy is expected to be published.
answer_text: Officials from my department are currently in the process of finalising the Circular Economy Strategy. It is my intention to bring it to the Executive in the coming weeks for approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any consideration he has given to the introduction of scalp cooling treatments to support those with chemotherapy-induced alopecia.
answer_text: Cold caps/scalp cooling treatments are not routinely commissioned in HSC (NI).  I am advised that they are not part of the NICE recommended care pathway for treatment of chemotherapy/SACT-induced alopecia hair loss.  The routine commissioning of this treatment can only be considered based on the long-term evidence of efficacy, available funding and the relative priority of this service in the context of other service development proposals and the overall HSC financial position.The commissioning position on Scalp Cooling can be reviewed on receipt of UK evidence review and provided this is in line with NICE recommendations for cancer patients.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the development of the Net Zero Accelerator Fund in collaboration with the Strategic Investment Board.
answer_text: The Net Zero Accelerator Fund is a commitment in the Programme for Government and is being developed by Invest NI on behalf of DfE.  Invest NI are currently progressing the business case for the fund. The fund has a target to launch in 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on when the three year Skills Action Plan will be published.
answer_text: The Skills Action Plan will be finalised once the draft budget is published.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to her intention to publish a three year Skills Action Plan, how it will be integrated with, and complement, the existing (i) Digital Skills Action Plan; (ii) sectoral action plans; and (iii) skill up initiatives.
answer_text: The Skills Action Plan will be compliant with and take account of the need to implement the actions contained within the Digital Skills Action Plan, the people and skills theme of the Department’s sectoral plans and other relevant skills initiatives under the Department’s vires.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the three year Careers Action Plan, in collaboration with the Department of Education, will be published.
answer_text: Officials from both Departments have worked collaboratively to develop a joint Careers Action Plan which will progress enhanced delivery across the full careers landscape and in partnership with a range of stakeholders. This plan is at an advanced stage of development. Submission of the draft Action Plan to both Ministers is anticipated over the coming weeks. The joint Careers Action Plan is cross-cutting and will require Executive referral prior to publishing.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any work he has undertaken in recent months to improve access to GPs.
answer_text: I recognise the importance of access to GP services for patients.  As you will be aware, in response to increasing patient demand GPs are utilising a mixed consultation model with telephone, video and face to face consultations as a means of managing the rising demand on services within the existing workforce while retaining responsiveness to those patients who need the most urgent attention and care. As independent contractors, who provide primary medical services to their registered list of patients under the terms of the General Medical Services Contracts Regulations, and clinical professionals, GPs are responsible for making appropriate decisions about the treatment, care and management of individual patients.  This also means that decisions about the day to day running of the surgery, including the use of technology, telephony and the appointment system, are determined locally by the GP practice within the constraints of their available workforce.There are approximately 200k consultations across general practice in Northern Ireland each week, with over 50% of those delivered face to face.Additional measures have been put in place to support primary care to adapt flexibly to increasing demand.  These include maintaining GP training place numbers at an historic high level of 121, additional investment in general practice and increasing the range of healthcare professionals working in primary care.The recently announced allocation of £61m of Transformation funding to the Primary Care MDT Programme will enable completion of the model in the 7 existing MDT areas, and expansion into 5 new GP Federation areas with a population of around 670,000 people, over the next 4 years to the end of 2028/29.The new areas to benefit from transformation funding are North Belfast, the South-West (Fermanagh/ West Tyrone), East Antrim, Craigavon and Dungannon/ Armagh.My Department has also established a GP Access Working Group to explore issues relating to access to services and develop a programme of work to help address these and improve the experience for patients. The Working Group includes representatives from General Practice and service users among its membership.  The GP Access Working Group is currently overseeing development of a suite of guidance to cover good practice in demand management, workflow optimisation and standardisation of processes.  The Group is also considering how technology can help to deliver improvements to the process for accessing services for patients and practices alike.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps he can take to improve the number of GPs serving patients in Carryduff, given the increase in population in the area in recent years.
answer_text: I recognise the ongoing challenges facing General Practice and the impact that increasing demand and workload pressures are having on GPs.To help primary care to deliver and grow, it is critical we train, develop and retain the GP workforce. Despite the numbers of GPs here increasing by around 9 per cent between 2018 and 2023, this has been offset by changing patterns of GP work.The number of GP training places was previously increased to 121, an increase of 86% since 2015/16. The number of places will be retained at this level for 2024/25. Additional measures have been put in place to support primary care to adapt flexibly to increasing demand. These include focusing on increasing capacity to meet the demand from patients through, for example, reinforcing the GP workforce and increasing the range of healthcare professionals working in primary care through multidisciplinary teams.My Department has worked with key stakeholders, to increase the exposure of undergraduates to General Practice; encourage more GP trainees; and ensure that there are sufficient GP training practices to meet demand and support GPs in training.  GP Fellowship Schemes are supporting recently qualified GPs to transition from the training environment to independent practice, with opportunities for personal and professional development. The Attract, Recruit, Retain scheme supports recruitment of GPs in hard to recruit areas. Since its establishment in January 2023, 83 GP practices have applied and received approval in principle for funding under the scheme.  53 practices have applied and received final approval for payment in respect of a recruited GP.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he will make administrative boundary Shapefiles, held by NISRA, of (i) 1985 and 1973 ward boundaries; (ii) 1985 and 1973 district electoral area boundaries; (iii) 1985 and 1973 local government district boundaries; (iv) 1995, 1982 and 1970 parliamentary constituencies; and (v) any other administrative boundaries, available to the public for free via Open Data NI and Spatial NI.
answer_text: ANSWERNISRA does not hold administrative boundary shapefiles for all the boundaries stated in the request but has access to the following relevant historic shapefiles: 1985 wards (labelled ‘wards1984’) 1985 local government district boundaries (labelled ‘dc84’) 1995 parliamentary constituencies (labelled ‘PC1995_region’) Given the historic nature of these shapefiles and the undocumented data quality, NISRA does not plan to publish them on Open Data NI or Spatial NI. NISRA may release these files to individual requestors on request.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work he is undertaking with the Minister for the Economy to tackle economic inactivity amongst women in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My department has received £145,000 of funding from the Department for  Economy (DfE) to deliver a range of provision in support of the Childcare sector through our Labour Market Partnerships (LMPs). It is recognised that lack of affordable childcare can be a barrier for people with dependants who are considering returning to work or increasing their working hours.This funding allows participants to engage in Childcare Employment Academies, aiming to assist them to gain new qualifications, and move into employment/self-employment or higher paid employment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any support she is providing to support upskill programmes to tackle economic inactivity among women.
answer_text: My department provides a range of programmes, through our skills and education remit, which are designed to support women, and those from other under-represented groups who face barriers to employment.  This is a key feature of the Skills Fund that my colleague Conor Murphy launched last July with an investment of almost £12m, facilitating skills interventions.  Our Step Up Project delivers a Women Returners Access Programme (WRAP) delivered across our Further Education colleges to support women in building confidence, gain skills, and prepare for employment opportunities.  Funding has been provided for a series of Women Returners initiatives to help individuals use learning to gain the confidence and skills to re-enter the workforce.  The programme provides skills and training for up to 200 women, with the potential for a work placement and access to employment opportunities.We also have All Age Apprenticeships in place now, as well as an Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund to increase apprenticeship uptake and encouraging those from under-represented groups, including women, to consider apprenticeships as an upskilling opportunity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps she has taken to engage with employers on creating more inclusive workplaces for those with disabilities.
answer_text: Staff in my Employer Services Branch provide direct support to employers of all sizes across Northern Ireland to address their recruitment needs. They promote the use of my Department's employment support programmes such as the Condition Management Programme, Access to Work and Workable NI with employers.  These programmes have been specifically developed to enable people with health conditions and disabilities to find, retain and progress in employment. They also encourage employers to consider flexibility in their recruitment practices as well as helping them fulfil their social value obligations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she has had with the Minister for Communities on the development of the disability employment strategy.
answer_text: My officials have worked with colleagues in the Department for Communities (DfC) throughout the development and co-design of the Disability and Work Strategy, formally known as the Disability Employment Strategy. My department has ownership of approximately one third of the draft actions and commitments currently contained within the Strategy, under the theme of ‘Inclusive Skills, Careers, and Educational Transitions’.My officials will continue their close working relationship with DfC colleagues throughout the ongoing development, approval, and implementation of the Strategy.I look forward to engaging with Minister Lyons and Executive colleagues upon receipt of the final draft.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any steps her Department is taking to support more people with autism into the workplace.
answer_text: I recognise that many learners with special educational needs, including those who are autistic, face greater barriers at the point of leaving school and transitioning onto appropriate education, training and employment pathways. I am committed to taking action to address this issue and I expect to receive a report shortly from my officials who have been examining post-school provision for young people with special educational needs. My Department offers a range of skills provision which include measures to support individuals who face additional barriers, including autism, to enable them to build the skills and qualifications needed to compete for employment opportunities at all levels. Amongst these measures are Breaking Barriers in education, Skill Up and Step Up’s Disability Access Route to Employment and Education (DARE). Additionally, targeted support for Widening Participation into higher education is provided through interventions and financial support for several target groups, including those with a disability (physical, sensory or learning) including Autism. My Department’s Careers Service is also working to develop and introduce additional interactive technology which can address the specific issues autistic people face when trying to access careers information and advice.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any engagement the independent reviewer of the school curriculum has had with industry in relation to the impact of Artificial Intelligence on future skills needs of the Northern Ireland economy.
answer_text: To date, the review engaged with a range of business and industry representatives, and other stakeholders. This engagement has raised a number of relevant issues, including the importance of digital skills, the potential impact of AI and the future needs of the Northern Ireland economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to the UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology Artificial Intelligence sector study 2023, whether she will commission a study of Artificial Intelligence business demographics in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: As part of the work of the AI Collaboration Centre (AICC), funded by my Department and Invest NI, a baseline study will be carried out, which will include an in-depth AI ecosystem review of the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will commission research into the potential impact of Artificial Intelligence on the Northern Ireland labour market.
answer_text: As part of the ongoing foresight work of the MATRIX panel to inform and advise my Department, it will be commissioning research to look at Artificial Intelligence and the future of work in the North. The results of the research are expected to be published this autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how her Department is seeking to tackle age discrimination in job interviews.
answer_text: Employers have a duty under the Employment Equality (Age) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 to ensure that they do not treat an individual less favourably on any grounds related to their age, including in job interviews.  This legislation is the responsibility of the Department for Communities.My Department is responsible for the Labour Relations Agency (LRA) and it tackles recruitment related age discrimination through the provision of published recruitment and discrimination advice, guidance and best practice, along with various webinars and training events that specifically tackle this issue. The LRA also provides a conciliation service that can help those who believe they have been treated less favourably by employers on grounds relating to age to conciliate a related dispute. My Department also provides funding and Secretariat support for the Office of Industrial Tribunals and the Fair Employment Tribunal (OITFET). These tribunals are independent judicial bodies that decide claims about employment, including where an individual feels that they have been treated less favourably at a job interview in relation to their age.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) a timeline for the establishment of the proposed Office of AI and Digital; and (ii) the resources that will be allocated to the office.
answer_text: We have established an Office of AI and Digital and tasked the Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser (CSTA) with chairing a Task-and-Finish Group comprising representatives from government departments, industry and academia to scope out the remit of the Office. The Task-and-Finish Group will present recommendations regarding the remit and requirements for the Office of AI and Digital, as well as a delivery plan. Resource allocation will be considered as part of this process.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how the proposed Office of AI and Digital will operate in collaboration with (i) all government departments; (ii) other public sector bodies; (ii) academia; and (iv) industry, to deliver tangible benefits of Artificial Intelligence for our economy and society.
answer_text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and data-driven technologies are increasingly central to modern governance, economic growth, and social well-being. Governments worldwide are adopting AI capabilities to enhance public services, drive innovation, and strengthen resilience against emerging challenges. In Northern Ireland, there is a growing AI ecosystem spanning government, academic research, and private-sector activity.  That is why we are creating an AI Unit.To ensure strategic alignment, transparency, and effective outcomes, we have tasked the Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser with chairing a Task-and-Finish Group comprising representatives from government departments, industry and academia to scope out the remit of, and objectives for, an Office of AI and Digital, as set out in the PfG the first meeting took place in  March 2025. Recognising AI’s transformative power and the need for responsible stewardship, the Group will provide leadership, technical insights, and cross-sector coordination across five workstream pillars: AI Governance; AI Infrastructure; Data; AI Skills; and Public Sector Transformation. Work will then commence on the development of an AI Action Plan on the basis of what is identified through the Task-and-Finish Group.The Task-and-Finish Group will be an opportunity for all government departments, other public sector bodies, academia, and industry to collaborate to develop an AI Action Plan to deliver tangible benefits for the NI economy and society.Our Chief Scientific and Technology Advisor is already working with a range of key stakeholders to develop a regional strategy for Science and Technology to drive this work forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration that has been given to the provision of concessionary fares for carers.
answer_text: The NI Concessionary Fares Consultation ran from 1st June 2023 to 24th August 2023 and included an option which would introduce companion passes for disabled people unable to travel alone. Under this proposal, disabled people who met the eligibility criteria would be issued with a SmartPass which indicates that they are allowed to have a companion travel with them on their journey. This companion pass would allow the companion to receive concessionary travel for that journey, thereby benefitting some carers.Although public support for this option was strong (with 79% of respondents in favour of the change), unfortunately, due to the severe financial pressures facing the Department, changes which would increase the cost of the Scheme could not be implemented at that time.While the budgetary outlook has not changed, I will reconsider the qualifying criteria again in the future, if the financial position allows.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on when the (i) footpaths; and (ii) road at Onslow Gardens, Belfast, will be resurfaced.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and this has had a significant impact on its ability to deliver essential services such as road maintenance. There are many roads that would undoubtedly benefit from investment, including those in Onslow Gardens, however, due to a limited budget, resurfacing work must be taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need of intervention.Officials are currently developing the 2025/26 resurfacing programme, and this will be provided in the Department’s Annual Report to Belfast City Council.In the interim, my officials will continue to undertake regular inspections in Onslow Gardens and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s current maintenance policy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any actions their Department is taking to tackle racism in South Belfast, given their responsibilities for racial equality and good relations.
answer_text: We, and the Executive as a whole, strongly condemn racism in all its forms. As recent events have shown, racism has a devastating impact on victims, their families and our society. Whilst the immediate focus of our Department has been on making sure our communities and the people who live in them are safe, we must now focus on how we can tackle racism and hate as part of our wider good relations policies.To support this, TEO provides funding of over £7million to local organisations and councils to support cohesion and integration, address good relations issues and reduce the potential for young people to engage in negative or anti-social behaviour.Alongside our review of the T:BUC strategy, we have commenced work on the development of a new strategic approach to racial equality post 2025 and expect a key focus of this to be tackling racism and hate. We have taken learning from other programmes which deal with societal issues and  our focus is on preventative actions and effective interventions to change attitudes and behaviours.We aim to launch a public consultation on a draft strategy later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions she has had with the UK Government in relation to the delivery of 5,000 air defence missiles to Ukraine.
answer_text: I have held no discussions with the British Government in relation to the delivery of 5,000 air defence missiles to Ukraine.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the implementation and development of the £150 million investment zone.
answer_text: My officials continue to work closely with the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG) to co-design the North’s £150m Enhanced Investment Zone programme. The development of the Programme must follow an MHCLG pre-agreed staged approval process, the target for final approval is Autumn 2025. Key to securing approval at each stage is clear demonstration that Investment Zone funding will drive productivity and leverage significant private sector funding. During March my Department will be hosting regional workshops for further engagement with the private sector and other stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) any overseas trade missions she intends to engage with over the remainder of the 2025 calendar year; and (ii) the rationale for each of those visits.
answer_text: Currently I have no confirmed travel plans to any events. An indicative schedule of overseas events in which ministerial representation may be appropriate and advantageous is under consideration and discussion with Invest NI’s Trade Division.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for her assessment of the impact of the cuts to the budget for Queen's University Belfast; and (ii) how these cuts will impact on the ability of the Executive to support greater investment in research and development.
answer_text: All Departmental budgets are under huge pressure. Therefore, areas across my Department have been asked to assess the impact of a reduction in their baseline budget. The budget remains in draft form until conclusion of the equality impact assessment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the progress of the scoping exercise being undertaken to understand the business support needs of the early years and childcare sector.
answer_text: The research is progressing at pace. Consultations have taken place with individual childcare providers and parents as well as a cross-section of stakeholder and representative organisations within the Childcare and wider sectors. The information gathered is now being analysed and the research remains on schedule to complete by the end of March. The resultant evidence base will allow my Department to move quickly to make recommendations to the Minister of Education on the nature of assistance required by Childcare providers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any consideration that has been given by his Department to the use of Artificial Intelligence to improve the efficiency and efficacy of the rates collection processes.
answer_text: As a large-scale operational delivery area, collecting over £1.5 billion rates, intelligent automation is widely used in rate collection activities for processes such as filling in forms, issuing correspondence and workflow management. In considering the use of Artificial Intelligence, Land and Property Services adheres to the General Data Protection Regulations on the use of Generative AI. These regulations protect the rights of individuals not to be subject to solely automated decisions, including profiling, that have a legal or similarly significant effect on them.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the engagement mechanisms that exist between scientific advisors in different Executive departments.
answer_text: Professor Helen McCarthy was appointed as Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser (CSTA) to the Executive in June 2024. As CSTA, Prof. McCarthy convenes monthly meetings with the Scientific Advisers in DAERA and DoH. These meetings are supplemented by ad-hoc meetings when required. The CSTA chairs the NI Science and Technology Advisory Network (NISTAN) which has representation from all Executive Departments to assist the CSTA in championing the use of science and technology to create a stronger evidence base for policy development and decision-making.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any consideration that has been given to trialling the use of Artificial Intelligence for triaging and booking GP appointments.
answer_text: As independent contractors, and clinical professionals, GPs are responsible for making appropriate decisions about the treatment, care and management of individual patients.  This also means that decisions about the day to day running of the surgery, including the use of technology, telephony and the appointment system, are determined locally by the GP practice within the constraints of their available workforce.As GPs utilise commercial software suppliers to handle Practice Management and Patient Records Management, these suppliers would be best placed to consider how the integration of AI based tools into their existing solutions could assist GPs in the delivery of their work. There would however need to be extensive consideration and evaluation of cybersecurity and information governance controls that would need to be provided in any future AI tool, as there is the need to assure public that their information is correctly protected.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any consideration that has been given to the development of a research institute to advise Executive departments on policy development that would operate in a similar fashion to the Wales Centre for Public Policy.
answer_text: The NICS’ guide to policy work, “Making a Difference”, highlights that the involvement of partners is critical to person-centered policy, and strongly encourages it in considering and advising on what works best.  Policy officials in Departments regularly engage with external sources of information, insight and ideas, including universities, and foster close relationships with researchers, practitioners, and representative bodies.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any action she can take to ease congestion along the A55 from the Milltown Road towards the junction with the Saintfield Road, South Belfast.
answer_text: My officials are not aware of any particular congestion issues along the A55 Milltown Road, Saintfield Road corridor over and above those experienced by similar roads within the Belfast area during peak periods. Whilst there are no plans to carry out major changes along this route, I can advise that a scheme to resurface the Purdysburn Road is due to commence in March 2025. My Department is actively seeking to empower people to make more sustainable choices, particularly for shorter everyday journeys and in combination with public transport for longer journeys.  With safe, reliable, and accessible active travel infrastructure, individuals are more likely to embrace healthier alternatives to driving, in turn reducing congestion in our towns and cities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 14634/22-27, for an update on the progress of the formal procurement competition.
answer_text: The procurement process commenced on the scheme on 12 December 2024 with the advertisement of the prequalification phase. Applications from interested contractors have been received and are currently being assessed.The tender phase is scheduled to commence in May 2025 with award anticipated in February 2026, subject to final business case approval and the outcome of departmental budget settlements.The scheme is expected to commence on site in March 2026, with a 3 year construction period, completing in Spring 2029.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on plans to establish an Artificial Intelligence Unit in their Department.
answer_text: We are creating an AI Unit and we have tasked the Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser with chairing a Task-and-Finish Group comprising representatives from government departments, industry, and academia to scope out the remit of, and requirements for, an Office of AI and Digital for the region, as set out in the PfG. The first meeting took place in March 2025. The Task-and-Finish Group will consider a number of proposed workstream pillars, including public sector reform; governance; skills; data and infrastructure. Work will then commence on the development of an AI strategy on the basis of what is identified through the Task-and-Finish Group.A significant part of the work will look at how we integrate and showcase the expertise we already have, and how we progress the areas that need to be developed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 21711/22-27, whether she has been briefed by officials about the scope and approach of the review.
answer_text: In my response to AQW 21711/22-27 on 7th February 2025 I outlined that officials were currently engaging with the sector as regards to the potential scope and approach to the Review of Higher Education Funding.  The engagement meetings with the sector concluded on 18th February 2025 when officials met with NUS-USI.  As the sector engagement meetings have now concluded, officials are working on briefing which I expect to receive imminently.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 9445/22-27 and AQW 22239/22-27, why there has been such a delay to beginning this review.

To ask the Minister of Health whether the 20 per cent increase in student maintenance loans from 2025-26, recently announced by the Minister for the Economy, will have any impact on the support available to students on Allied Health Professional courses funded by his Department.
answer_text: I am currently exploring with my officials possible future changes to the financial support provided to students on all pre-registration healthcare programmes, including AHPs, commissioned by my Department. Any changes will not come into effect before the 2025/26 academic year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the operation of the (i) Irish Language Broadcast Fund; and (ii) Ulster-Scots Broadcast Fund.
answer_text: The Irish Language Broadcast Fund supports Irish language content of high quality and fosters the Irish speaking independent production sector in the North. The Ulster Scots Broadcast Fund supports expression of heritage, culture and language through moving image. The Fund also aims to foster the Ulster-Scots independent production sector here and to fund high quality Ulster-Scots cultural TV and radio programmes. Both Funds are administered by NI Screen and funding is provided by the Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport via the British Film Institute. The Irish Language Broadcast Fund also receives funding from the Irish Government via the Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sports and Media. Funding for 2024/25 is provided in the table below.	FUND	2024/25 Budget		Source	Irish Language Broadcast Fund	£3.2m	Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport 	Irish Language Broadcast Fund	€1.0m	Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sports and Media	Ulster-Scots Broadcast Fund	£1.1m 	Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any mitigations that can be put in place to prevent traffic congestion during the simultaneous ongoing works by Northern Ireland Electricity Networks on the Ballymaconaghy Road and the closure of lanes on the Knockbracken Road, South Belfast.
answer_text: I can advise that officials are currently dealing with a number of developer-led and utility works in the Carryduff area.  The developer-led road improvement scheme at the junction of A24 Saintfield Road and Knockbracken Road commenced on Tuesday 6 August 2024 and is programmed to be completed by July 25.Officials have recently been in discussions with NIE about 600m of cable laying on the Ballymaconaghy Road to provide service connections and to upgrade their network.On balance, officials agreed to the works starting to allow them to meet connection targets and due to the possible knock-on effect of more major works which are likely to take place on Ballymaconaghy Road during this summer. While the works are programmed to last four weeks, officials did delay NIE from starting until works at an adjacent housing site on Ballymaconaghy Road had progressed to a stage where the two works did not impact on each other. It is however appreciated that there remains a level of conflict with the works the Saintfield Road/Knockbracken Road junction.Please be assured that every effort is made to coordinate and plan roadworks to minimise the impact on road users. However, given the number, scale and complexity of these projects, some overlap is unavoidable.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any work undertaken in his Department to understand the impact of racism on the mental health of ethnic minority communities in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Two briefing papers (Health Intelligence Briefs, {HIBs}) were prepared by PHA facilitated working groups and are as follows: The HIB on ethnic minorities summarised the evidence at the time (up to 2012) on racist incidences (PSNI data), public attitudes/prejudice towards ethnic minorities (based on the NI Life and Times Survey) and experiences of racism/discrimination at work, when accessing healthcare, and those in prison within the frame of wider determinants of health. For children/young people, experiences of discrimination/racist bullying in schools were also noted, drawing on multiple research projects conducted in the preceding years.  The statistics cited have changed since but can be accessed from their respective sources: PSNI Hate Motivation Statistics | PSNI and the NILT NI Life and Times Survey - Attitudes to Minority Ethnic People. The HIB on Travellers’ mental health (2012) provided an overview of prevalence of mental health issues as well as the role of experiences of discrimination/racism in the lives of Travellers as an influence on their mental health. This covered the impact of discrimination during childhood and adult years. It drew extensively on the All Ireland Traveller Health Study but also on local research/engagement in NI.The PHA (Health Intelligence) also commissioned a report and executive summary specifically regarding asylum seekers/refugees, exploring options to support their Mental Health on request from Health Improvement colleagues. The report on refugee and asylum seekers’ mental health (2018) highlighted racism/discrimination as one post-migration stressor that can lead to social isolation and poor mental health. Overall, it is the same factors that impact the mental health of the host population that also influence the mental health of asylum seekers/refugees, though the latter are more likely to experience more risk and fewer protective factors. The report explored, across a wide range of influencing factors, projects to improve/maintain mental health among asylum seekers/refugees as examples of good practice.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of how infrastructure could better support the development of sustainable tourism.
answer_text: My Department launched its Tourism Vision and Action Plan: 10 Year Plan in January this year. It contains 17 actions across the Department’s four objectives of Net Zero, Good Jobs, Regional Balance and Productivity. It includes actions to build the capacity of tourism businesses to become more sustainable and to promote good practice in building sustainable tourism. How our visitors move around and interact with our region is critical not only to the carbon footprint they leave behind, but also the memories they take with them. The Vision and Action Plan recognises that ‘…a seamless, hassle-free experience, including ease of getting around the region from the moment visitors arrive until they leave is essential to creating a world-class visitor experience and more likely to encourage repeat visits and longer stays. Improvement in our transport infrastructure also leads to safer travel and connected communities’. The Department for Infrastructure has representation on the Tourism Partnership Board which oversees the implementation of the Action Plan. Its work on developing transport plans together with maintaining and strengthening our transport system is crucial for meeting the travel experience needs of our visitors and citizens. A well-integrated and sustainable transport network not only improves accessibility to key attractions but also minimizes the environmental impact of travel. By future proofing and investing in our transport system, destinations can attract more visitors, reduce congestion, and promote local economies. The Department for Infrastructure is committed to increasing opportunities for Active Travel and will continue to work in partnership with key stakeholders in the public and private sectors to develop active travel infrastructure that encourages walking, wheeling and cycling as an attractive alternative to the private car. In addition, as well as providing a number of seasonal services to enable connectivity by public transport to tourist sites, Translink has ongoing engagement with Tourism NI to discuss strategic priorities and identify mutual opportunities to add value.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work underway to encourage and support the engagement of social enterprises in public procurement processes.
answer_text: My Department recently published a Social Enterprise Action Plan in which procurement was identified as a key area of focus for the sector. Officials are undertaking work to raise commissioning awareness within DfE to support social enterprises in delivering social value.In November 2024, the social enterprise co-design group, along with Social Enterprise NI and their Board members, met with officials from Central Procurement Directorate, who presented on the Procurement Act 2023 and outlined the opportunities for social enterprises through procurement or grant funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 22809/22-27, to detail the (i) grade; and (ii) job title of the lead official for each of the seven sectoral action plans published by her Department.
answer_text: The delivery of the sectoral action plans requires coordination across DfE, Invest NI, wider government, academia and industry. To lead this my Department recently established a new Trade, Innovation and Priority Sectors Division within the Economic Policy Group. The Division is led at G5, Assistant Secretary level, by Alan Ramsey.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work her Department has undertaken in relation to developing stewardship plans for priority tourism destinations.
answer_text: Developing a Destination Stewardship approach is one of the key priorities in the Tourism Vision & Action Plan: 10 Year Plan, launched on 15 January 2025. This will include a Stewardship framework and principles to be used as a blueprint to develop tourism destinations sustainably across the North in the future. Tourism NI is currently in the process of procuring a suitably qualified and experienced contractor to co-create the Destination Stewardship Framework. The specialist contractor will collaborate with Tourism NI and all councils to gather research, data and evidence to define destinations and select one for the pilot Destination Stewardship Plan.  Following this process, my Department will be in a position to advise on the identified destination selected for the pilot. My Department anticipates that Tourism NI will have completed their work on the inital stage in September 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Stranmillis Embankment pedestrian and cycling improvement scheme, Belfast.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide an update on any advice they have received from officials on their review of work that was previously undertaken to extend equality legislation to include age discrimination for the provision of goods and services.
answer_text: No one in our society should be discriminated against because of their age. TEO officials have recently submitted advice on this matter which is currently under review.The Junior Ministers met with Claire Sugden MLA to discuss her proposed Age Goods, Facilities and Services Bill and the challenges in bringing forward legislation on this issue. Development of any age discrimination laws will require careful consideration and work to inform their scope, as well as required exemptions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 21423/22-27, to detail (i) whether his Department would recognise Shapefiles of historic local government boundaries as an authoritative representation of those boundaries where such boundaries were produced by an organisation other than the Ordnance Survey Northern Ireland; and (ii) the circumstance under which recognition would be granted.
answer_text: My Department has responsibility for historical maps and makes available the six inch historical maps for 1829-1986 on its Historical Maps Viewer. The recognition of formats or representations of historical boundaries does not fall within the remit of the Public Records Act.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any consideration given to the implementation of the GoodSAM software for emergency call centres.

To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to the Software and Cyber Action Plan, how her Department is working with (i) universities; (ii) the further education sector; and (iii) industry partners to grow the numbers of people obtaining computing-related skills and qualifications.
answer_text: The Software and Cyber Action Plan is a 3-year plan with a range of activities which will help grow the sector. Skills is an important theme across this action plan. My officials continue to engage with key stakeholders across industry and academia to help grow the number of people obtaining computing-related skills and qualifications.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail the criteria used to allocate the most recent £1.8 million tranche of the Core Grant Funding Scheme 2025-26; and (ii) for his assessment of the value of the work that has been carried out by those groups that were unsuccessful in their applications for funding.
answer_text: The Core Grant Funding Scheme for 2025-26 was co-designed in 2024 with Community and Voluntary Sector representatives, and included the following scoring criteria:Evidence of how an organisation supports the department’s strategic objectives;Evidence that an organisation is involved in carrying out relevant activities;Evidence that an organisation will deliver clear outcomes, including how those will be measured and the targets that will be met;Evidence that an organisation has delivered similar outcomes previously; Evidence that an organisation has suitable monitoring and control systems in place, and suitably qualified and experienced staff; andEvidence that an organisation will work in partnership across the statutory, community and voluntary sectors. These scoring criteria are listed and explained in the guidance for applicants, available on the departmental website.The high standard of applications has reinforced the valuable contribution that voluntary and community sector organisations make towards health and social care outcomes. The selection of the 25 highest scoring applications to progress to stage 2 of the assessment process was based solely on agreed scoring criteria, rather than a departmental determination of the value of the applicants. My officials will be in contact with applicants in the coming weeks to offer feedback, which I hope will be of assistance in applying for future funding rounds. Indeed, those who have missed out this time will have another opportunity later this year to bid for funding in 2026/27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, considering the availability of images on the NISRA website of 1985 Wards, whether NISRA or any other public body under his remit, excluding Ordnance Survey of Northern Ireland, produced these images or any Shapefiles, images or any other form of digitised boundary data, for any purpose, for (i) the 1985 Wards; and (ii) for any other administrative boundaries in use between 1973 and 1997.
answer_text: The information requested is provided below. (i) the 1985 WardsNISRA holds a shapefile for the 1985 Wards;(ii) for any other administrative boundaries in use between 1973 and 1997 NISRA produces statistics on a wide variety of topics, often reporting them for a range of administrative boundaries (e.g. Local Government Districts, Health and Social Care Trusts). To complement the release of some statistics NISRA also produces supporting material such as statistical bulletins, images, infographics and presentations – which can include maps of administrative boundaries to add context.  In some cases, NISRA’s statistics are released in digital format within interactive applications that enable spatial analysis/ representation which also incorporate these administrative boundaries. In order to facilitate this, NISRA primarily sources shapefiles for these administrative boundaries from Ordnance Survey Northern Ireland (OSNI) through the Northern Ireland Mapping Agreement. OSNI is the only authoritative public body within my Department with remit over administrative boundaries, and NISRA is the only other public body within my Department with business remit or reason to extract and/or derive boundary data/information, in any spatial format, from OSNI mapping.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to the Software and Cyber Sectoral Action Plan, to detail any action taken to (i) research global best practice in computing education from primary through to tertiary levels; (ii) assess the applicability of initiatives and policies to the Northern Ireland context; and (iii) implement pilots of the most promising approaches.
answer_text: My Department has recently developed the Digital Skills Action Plan (2024 – 2034) which outlines challenges and actions that we as a Department, along with Department of Education and industry will undertake to improve digital skills. This includes creating alternative pathways into the digital sector. My officials are working closely alongside Software NI to ensure initiatives and policies remain applicable to industry needs here in the North. My Department along with Department of Education will continue to work to examine the barriers to teaching computer science within the education system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether any funding has been provided by Invest NI to support companies engaged in the (i) construction; and (ii) supply of weapons to the State of Israel.
answer_text: Invest NI does not support projects that supply arms to Israel.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the work of the Samaritans in supporting mental health and suicide prevention in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Department of Health appreciates the valuable contribution which Samaritans have made to mental health and suicide prevention in Northern Ireland for many years. The Samaritans make valuable contributions to the Protect Life 2 Steering Group and have developed best practice guidance for a range of issues such as media guidance for sensitive reporting of suicide. The Samaritans listening ear service is not funded by this Department however is very highly regarded by service users and they provide a first point of contact for many people in distress or despair.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the work of the US Net Zero Advisory Board.
answer_text: The US Net Zero Advisory Board was launched on 1st October 2024 with the support of Joe Kennedy III. The Board is comprised of leaders in Net Zero industries who bring immense knowledge and expertise and are tasked with enhancing international awareness of the North’s green economy and Net Zero strengths. The Board is already adding value for local companies by providing insights on the US market, making introductions and helping to raise the profile of the North’s green economy. Invest NI’s green economy team continues to proactively engage with the Board, with the most recent Board meeting held on 20th February 2025.  The Board is expected to play a crucial role in fostering collaboration, with ongoing discussions focused on the most effective strategies to strengthen international partnerships. Efforts are also aimed at positioning the North as a leading destination for global green investment while identifying new market opportunities both locally and internationally. Board members are engaged in a voluntary capacity based on their connection to the North and their willingness to support the growth of the North’s Green Economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of officials tasked with working on each of the seven sectoral action plans produced by her Department.
answer_text: It is not possible to specify the exact number of officials tasked with working on the seven sectoral action plans as the delivery of these plans is supported by a range of delivery partners across government, industry and academia.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the implementation of the Screen Industries Sectoral Action Plan.
answer_text: The Screen Industries Sectoral Action Plan is progressing well, with a focus on economic return, skills development, technological innovation and the celebration of our diverse culture and heritage. A notable milestone is that the first production to use the virtual production facilities in Studio Ulster is underway.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of any connections between Northern Ireland's low levels of regional research and development expenditure and our relatively low levels of productivity.
answer_text: Lower levels of investment in Research and Development is undoubtedly a factor in our relatively low productivity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in relation to plans to develop new electricity interconnection projects on a north-south and east-west basis, to detail how her Department is coordinating with industry and education partners to ensure we have the skills capacity in the system to deliver on these schemes.
answer_text: My Department is working with SONI and NIE Networks on delivery of the second North South Interconnector. This will improve the operation of the wholesale island electricity market by reducing inefficiency, increasing competition and improving security of supply here and bring material benefits to electricity consumers.  On the East-West interconnector, Transmission Investment has proposed a new interconnector (also known as LirIC), between the North and Scotland. In December last year the Utility Regulator granted a transmission license to LirIC. This is the first stage of a two-step process, and the Utility Regulator will now move to assess its proposed operating regime.  The soon to be launched Green Skills Action Plan has identified Infrastructure, which includes the electricity network, as a priority area. A industry led sub-group, which will have representation from the utilities sector as well as both higher and further education, is being established to look at the skills requirement for the priority sectors and advise the Department on the skills and timeline for delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) which Local Economic Partnerships have met since the previous Minister's announcement on boosting regional economic performance; (ii) when those meetings took place; and (iii) the outcomes from those meetings.
answer_text: Three Local Economic Partnerships (LEPs) have met to date in January and February 2025 – Antrim and Newtownabbey, Mid and East Antrim, and Newry Mourne and Down; Derry City and Strabane intend to meet by the end of February. Remaining councils are planning their Partnerships, with support from the Department and Invest NI, and are expected to meet in March, although in many cases this is dependent on council ratification processes.		Initial meetings have predominantly focused on logistics – discussing and agreeing the Terms of Reference, membership, what supporting data for the area is required, and ongoing scheduling. Further LEP meetings are progressing to map out their local economic priorities and subsequent areas of focus for potential projects to be funded for delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline the timeline for the delivery of a Deposit Return Scheme.
answer_text: I am very pleased to report that The Deposit Scheme for Drinks Containers (England and Northern Ireland) Regulations 2025 were made on 23rd January 2025. These regulations outline the framework for implementing the DRS, which is scheduled to commence in October 2027, these have now been published together with supporting evidence on www.gov.uk. With Scotland's own regulations also progressing, this marks a major step forward for the introduction of the scheme across the three nations.This followed the decision by Welsh Government to withdraw from the scheme in November 2024. However, as outlined at the time, I remain fully committed to DRS being implemented as soon as possible, so that the people of Northern Ireland will benefit from increased, high-quality recycling with reduced littering.The introduction of the new parliamentary regulations is a significant milestone and further signals our commitment to move forward together to deliver our goals to protect our climate, drive green growth and reduce unnecessary waste, whilst tackling the scourge of litter in our communities.The next stage will see the appointment of a Deposit Management Organisation (DMO), which will be an independent, not for profit and a private body responsible for the administration and day-to-day running of the scheme. The application process, developed across the 3 Nations, closed earlier this month, with an appointment expected by end of April 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the improvement works at Cairnshill park and ride facility will (i) begin; and (ii) be completed.
answer_text: The contractor carrying out the works at Cairnshill Park & Ride has confirmed that installation of the bollards will commence during the week of 17 February 2025 with completion within 2 to 3 weeks.The second phase, the installation of the barriers, will commence as soon as possible afterwards.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 13929/22-27; (ii) AQW 20041/22-27; and (iii) AQW 20043/22-27.

To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 21283/22-27, and in relation to the ongoing review of support for Department-funded students on Allied Health Professional courses, to detail any plans he has to increase bursaries for nursing students.
answer_text: The ongoing financial pressures facing my Department render unaffordable any increase in the bursary support for nursing and midwifery students. I am currently exploring with my officials possible future changes to the financial support provided to students on the pre-registration healthcare programmes commissioned by my Department, including in the nursing and midwifery fields.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the percentage increase in babies delivered at the Ulster Hospital Maternity Unit; and (ii) the percentage increase in doctors working within the same unit, in each of the last ten years.
answer_text: The public consultation exercise will commence in the coming weeks seeking views on adult: child ratios. Work will commence shortly in partnership with a stakeholder reference group to update the wider Minimum Standards document, with a view to consulting on and implementing a new set of Standards by December 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) how he intends to spend the £50 million he has been allocated by the Executive for Early Learning and Childcare; and (ii) whether this will include an allocation to fund an increase in the cap for the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme.
answer_text: I have secured £50m for Early Learning and Childcare provision in the draft 2025-26 Executive Budget, currently out for consultation.  I am considering options for its allocation, based on what is affordable.  When the budget is agreed, I will bring forward proposals to the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how he plans to incorporate the skills needs within our economy, identified by the Northern Ireland Skills Barometer 2022-23, in his review of the school curriculum.
answer_text: The review being undertaken independently by Lucy Crehan will make a series of policy recommendations regarding the purpose, design, specification and implementation of the Northern Ireland Curriculum. The review will of course take account of a wide range of research, including the Northern Ireland Skills Barometer.  To date, Lucy Crehan has engaged with business leaders including with those from the Northern Ireland Skills Council, the Institute of Directors and the Northern Ireland Chamber of Commerce. She has also met with Ulster University Economic Policy Centre, specifically in relation to the Skills Barometer.  As highlighted in the Skills Barometer there will be growth opportunities over the next 10 years across a range of sectors and occupations – with a focus predominantly on higher level skills and STEAM subjects.  It is vital that we support as many young people as possible to reach the education and qualification levels that will offer viable and sustainable opportunities in our emerging labour market.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 20499/22-27, to detail when the public consultation on adult to child ratios will be published.
answer_text: The public consultation exercise will commence in the coming weeks seeking views on adult: child ratios. Work will commence shortly in partnership with a stakeholder reference group to update the wider Minimum Standards document, with a view to consulting on and implementing a new set of Standards by December 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 20604/22-27, to detail any consideration given to the use of planters as an alternative means of traffic calming.
answer_text: The use of planters has not been considered as a method of traffic calming at Onslow Parade and Ravenhill Park, Belfast as without additional supporting measures, such as build-outs etc, they are not a suitable standalone method of controlling traffic. The initial cost of purchasing and ongoing maintenance costs would be prohibitive, and they may have potential to attract anti-social behaviour.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 20406/22-27, to detail any engagement her Department has had with the (i) UK Government; and (ii) the European Union in relation to Artificial Intelligence regulation.
answer_text: The regulatory frameworks to govern artificial intelligence are the responsibility of the British government and potentially the EU. Artificial Intelligence regulation featured as part of previous discussions between the Minister and the British government, including those on the AI Opportunities Action Plan. My department officials have also engaged with counterparts in DSIT on potential future British Government regulation.In addition to engaging with the British Government on the substance of the EU act, my officials have also emphasised to their counterparts the importance of providing NICS timely information on the parts of the act that the EU wishes to apply to the Windsor Framework under Article 13(4).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there are any plans to resurface Breda Drive, South Belfast.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years and this has had a significant impact on its ability to deliver essential services such as road maintenance. Whilst there are many roads that would undoubtedly benefit from investment, including Breda Drive, due to a limited budget, resurfacing work must be taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need of intervention.Officials are currently developing the 2025/26 resurfacing programme, and this will be provided in the Department’s Annual Report to Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council.In the interim, my officials will continue to undertake regular inspections of Breda Drive and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s current maintenance policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 20040/22-27, to detail any provision or support that will be put in place after people reach 18 years of age.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail any work he is undertaking, in conjunction with Executive colleagues, to address the high levels of economic inactivity due to ill-health.
answer_text: My Department works with other Executive Departments, including the Department for the Economy and Department for Communities, who lead on this important policy area, as well as local government and other collaborative partners, to help address this issue. Actions by my Department, through the Public Health Agency, to address high levels of economic inactivity due to ill-health are considered across the following policy areas:Supporting people to live healthier lives –Examples of actions in this area include:the development and roll out of a Whole Systems Approach to Obesity across each local Council area;implementation of the Mental Health Strategy;delivery of a Physical Activity Referral Scheme which supports people living with, or at risk of, a range of health conditions; and delivery of community-based health improvement programmes in areas of high economic inactivity, which promote smoking cessation and substance misuse support.Supporting healthier workplaces – Examples include:delivery of a regional Work Well Live Well programme, open to all employers across the region, which builds on the WHO best practice guidance for healthy workplaces;provision of workplace active travel programmes to support people to choose more sustainable travel options while adopting more physically active modes of travel; andthe Farm Families Programme which works in partnership with the  Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to support local farmers and their families to receive health checks and provide advice to improve their health and wellbeing.Supporting people towards employment –Examples include:provision of counselling services for people experiencing stress or anxiety;delivery of community-based learning programmes such as Men’s Sheds and Community Garden and Allotment programmes; andsupporting the development of Learning Cities with a focus on the role of learning as a tool to address inequalities and support people into formal and informal learning environments.Supporting collaboration between economic activity/employment and health – Examples include:working with local Councils and Labour Market Partnerships on economic inactivity and health initiatives; andengagement with colleagues in other regions to share learning and development of best practice to influence wider policy setting.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the role that the Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser will have in the development of a regional strategy for Artificial Intelligence.
answer_text: We are working to establish an AI Unit within The Executive Office as part of Reform and Transformation as envisaged in the Programme for Government.  This Unit will support public services adoption of AI technologies whilst ensuring they are used ethically and safely, and in a strategic way, addressing concerns around data protection, bias, and the environmental impact of AI. In addition, the AI Unit will ensure that Northern Ireland becomes a hub for AI research and innovation, attracting investment and fostering collaboration between industry, academia, and government.To progress this goal, a Task & Finish (T&F) Group has been established to scope out the remit and requirements for the proposed AI Unit. The T&F Group is chaired by the Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser. The group will bring together those from across departments, academia and industry, with expertise spanning data governance, AI ethics, digital transformation, real time use of data and public service delivery. A primary focus will be on identifying opportunities for leveraging AI to enhance decision-making, improve efficiency, and unlock value from public sector data assets.This work will contribute to the development of a Regional Strategy for Research and Innovation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 20233/22-27, to detail when a paper on Artificial Intelligence will be brought to the Executive.
answer_text: My predecessor as Economy Minister answered this question on 14 January 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans she has to expedite the completion of roadworks on the Knockbracken Road, South Belfast, given the disruption to local businesses.
answer_text: The developer-led road improvement scheme on the Knockbracken Road provides widening of the Saintfield Road to improve the junctions at Knockbracken Road, including the entrance to Knockbracken Healthcare Park.The scheme will also provide a much safer arrangement by the provision of dedicated right turn lanes, pedestrian facilities, cycling provision and road widening to maintain 4 lanes of carriageway. In addition, there will be an improved traffic signal layout and new bus stop laybys. The scheme is due to be completed by July 2025 and officials continue to work closely with the contractor to ensure this work is completed on time. Local residents will continue to be kept informed of progress and planned closure dates, with traffic management and variable messaging signage provided to minimise disruption and notify all road users of the closure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any consideration she has given to the development of a regional strategy for Artificial Intelligence for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I recognise the growing importance of AI for the economy with the technology’s potential to boost productivity, innovation and economic growth when safely and responsibly deployed.My officials are considering the whole AI landscape and the opportunities that exist to grow the region’s economy, building on the work that is already being undertaken by the AI Collaboration Centre; as well as considering the ambitious targets set out in the AI Action Plan recently launched by the British government.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to their responsibilities for integration, to detail any work they are undertaking to support the enhanced delivery of English for Speakers of Other Languages classes.
answer_text: We recognise the important role English for Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL) plays in supporting successful integration and welcome the work being led by the Department for the Economy (DfE) on the development of a draft ESOL Policy. This is being done in collaboration with stakeholders, including officials from The Executive Office.Over the last two financial years, we have also facilitated the allocation of additional funding to enhance ESOL provision. DfE has used this funding not only to provide additional ESOL classes, but to support the removal of barriers to ESOL and to develop bespoke ESOL provision, including ESOL with Childcare, the ESOL Transitions programme, aimed at young refugees, and ESOL for Employment. Funding has also been provided to local councils, some of which is intended for ESOL provision, and a number of Minority Ethnic Development Fund (MEDF) recipients choose to provide ESOL classes to those they work with.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to provide an update on the use of Artificial Intelligence across the Northern Ireland Civil Service and other public bodies.
To ask the Minister of Education to detail any consideration he has given to expanding the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme to cover school-aged childcare provision.
answer_text: I have secured £50m in the draft 2025-26 Executive Budget, currently out for consultation, for Early Learning and Childcare provision.   I am considering options, including what is affordable within the available allocation, to ensure the subsidy remains effective in reducing childcare costs for working parents.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail whether he will develop a childcare and early years workforce strategy to address recruitment and retention in the sector.
answer_text: I would refer the member to the answer to AQW 21808/22-27. The answer is contained in the Written Answers Booklet for week ending 7 February 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement her Department has had with the Department of Education to deliver the training and skills required to deliver school aged childcare.
answer_text: I am fully committed to working with the Minister for Education and other Executive colleagues to implement the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.My Department is working closely with the Department of Education, including representation on a range of related cross-departmental groups.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any work he is undertaking to address the issue of unregistered childcare.
answer_text: Unregistered childcare provision potentially put children at risk and also threatens the financial sustainability of registered provision in the area.
