answer_text: Asia Pacific: Headcount in the 2023-24 financial year was lower (i.e. two positions were not backfilled following staff movement including resignations, while another position was backfilled later in the financial year), as a result office overheads incurred were lower. Accompanied with a stronger GBP (£), the overall running costs reported (in GBP) for the Asia Pacific territory in 2023-24 was lower than the 2022-23 financial year.  India, Middle East and Africa: A reduction in headcount in the 2023-24 financial year (i.e. two positions were not backfilled) led to lower office overheads. A number of office subscriptions were not renewed in 2023-24, there were also recruitment costs incurred in 2022-23 but not in 2023-24. Accompanied with a stronger GBP (£), the overall running costs reported (in GBP) for the India, Middle East and Africa territory in 2023-24 was lower than the 2022-23 financial yearIn summary, the reduction in the 2023-24 running costs reported in GBP for both territories were due to staff movement (a reduction in headcount leading to lower office overheads) and exchange rate movement.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any engagement they have had with the (i) European Union; and (ii) UK Government in relation to the appointment of an EU Economic Envoy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: We have not had any engagement with the European Union or UK Government in relation to the appointment of an EU Economic Envoy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment of the potential value of having an EU Economic Envoy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: We have not received a proposal for an EU Economic Envoy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for an update on the operation of the Artificial Intelligence Collaboration Centre funded by this Department; and (ii) to detail the number of students enrolled for the 2024-25 academic year.
answer_text: (i) The Artificial Intelligence Collaboration Centre (AICC) was launched in March 2024.  It has since recruited a CEO and established a Governance Board, representing the views of industry stakeholders.  Since March, AICC has held 10 regional events and 100 industry meetings and engagements.  (ii) There is a total of 139 students enrolled in academic year 2024/25 on AICC Post Graduate scholarships across Ulster University and the Queen’s University.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in line with the commitment to expand the student numbers at the Ulster University Magee campus, to detail any discussions he has had with Executive colleagues to ensure the full range of community services are also enhanced to meet anticipated future demand.
answer_text: The Magee Expansion Taskforce Interim Report emphasises the need for a coordinated, cross-departmental approach to successfully expand student numbers at Ulster University's Magee campus. In light of this, I have written to my Executive colleagues, requesting their feedback and commitment to addressing the key Asks outlined in the report, which includes seeking an understanding of the potential impact on community services. I expect to receive responses in the coming days, which will be used to help shape the Taskforce's final report and action plan, for publication in December. The Department of Health, as a member of the Taskforce, will continue to play an active role in discussions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department is leading on retrofitting for homes.
answer_text: The retrofitting and the wider decarbonisation of homes is a cross-cutting issue with policy responsibility sitting across several departments and public bodies. My department engages closely with DfC, the Housing Executive and other stakeholders on these policy matters to ensure a coordinated approach to future policy interventions.  To facilitate this, a Residential Decarbonisation Coordination Group, jointly chaired by my Department and DfC officials, has been established to ensure effective cross Government collaboration on an approach for residential decarbonisation in line with the challenging targets contained within the Climate Change Act.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on how his Department is utilising Artificial Intelligence.
answer_text: My Department is not currently utilising Artificial Intelligence (AI) but is active on a NICS wide Digital Leaders’ Forum looking at AI use cases to take forward as proof of concept projects. We recently completed an exploratory project, undertaken via the Small Business Research Initiative (SBRI), to investigate technology to assist in measuring achievement in macro-economic indicators to drive coherent policy development and delivery. The suppliers who worked on this project considered how AI could be utilised, for example via Large Language Models (LLMs), to query documents, perform analysis, extract insights and provide written responses in natural language.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any consideration they have given to transferring responsibility for the implementation of the High Street Task Force Delivering a 21st Century High Street report to the Department for the Economy.
answer_text: The scope of the High Street Taskforce recommendations lie within the responsibilities of a number of departments and we are of the view that they should be taken forward by a cross-departmental group of the relevant departments, along with local councils. Therefore we wrote to Ministerial colleagues at the Department for Communities, Department for Economy, Department for Finance and Department for Infrastructure on 10th October 2024, asking them to consider the recommendations and determine the way forward, bringing matters to the Executive for consideration as necessary.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of pre-consultation meetings he held with (i) trade union representatives; and (ii) business organisations in relation to the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill public consultation.
answer_text: Prior to the launch of the public consultation on 1 July, I held one meeting where a range of trade union and business representatives were present.  I held a second meeting with a business representative organisation and its membership.I have also participated in number of relevant engagements during the consultation period.  Departmental officials have also facilitated or participated in a range of other engagements relating to the consultation, both before and during the consultation period.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 15909/22-27, to detail any progress that has been made towards growing external sales intensity to 60 per cent by 2030.
answer_text: In 2022 the North’s external sales intensity had grown to 51%, from the 2019 baseline of 45%. It is important to note there are many factors that are outside of the Department or indeed the Executive’s control which affects this measure, including exchange rates and global trade or demand patterns. Therefore, this is not a formal performance target, but a signal of ambition and a measure to provide insights into whether we are tracking in the right direction with my productivity agenda.Dual Market Access provides a significant opportunity for further growth in the EU market. I and Invest NI are working at pace to raise awareness of our unique position across Europe with potential buyers, including a series of planned events commencing in Germany this month.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the membership of the Careers Advisory Forum; and (ii) the number of meetings the group has held in the last 12 months.
answer_text: The Careers Advisory Forum (CAF) last met in November 2022. Following the resignation of the Chair, and in the absence of an Executive at Stormont, it was agreed that meetings should be paused, and that the forum’s future would be a matter for new Ministers.I have met with the Education Minister to discuss careers provision, including how we can enhance our strategic and operational partnerships. Officials in both Departments are currently considering the potential role of CAF to enable system level engagement as part of a reformed careers ecosystem.The full CAF membership can be found on the Department’s website: https://www.economy-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/economy/Careers-Advisory-Forum-members.pdf At the time of its last meeting CAF members represented the following groups:CCEA Education Authority ETI Assumption Grammar School, BallynahinchSERC Ulster University CBI NI Chambers of Commerce and Industry Federation of Small Business Business in the Community NI Committee Irish Congress of Trade Unions (NIC-ICTU) Parenting NI Disability Action Young Enterprise NI NICVA SOLACE While the CAF has been on hold, officials have been engaging stakeholders on early reform projects. For example, the Careers Portal Project hosted a workshop event in June 2024 which brought together over 30 key stakeholders from the careers ecosystem and included many CAF representatives.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the work he is carrying out to enable the 32 per cent of employers who said that they plan to offer apprenticeships in the future to do so in practice, as noted in the Employer Skills Survey 2022.
answer_text: Apprenticeships provide significant benefits to participants, employers and our economy. My Department is working on a range of approaches to grow and increase awareness of apprenticeships. There are two apprenticeship programmes, ApprenticeshipsNI and Higher Level Apprenticeships (HLAs), which means employers can access apprenticeships at all skill levels and in over 100 occupations.  Most recent published statistics for both programmes show that apprenticeship start numbers continue to increase.There is significant collaboration across our economy to ensure apprenticeships are relevant and align with our changing need. This is delivered by 16 sectoral partnerships bring together employers, qualification experts and policy advisors; and a yearly process to approve new apprenticeships.  Recent examples include new apprenticeships in Industrial Coatings and Scientific Technologies and new HLAs starting in Law and Artificial Intelligence.      My department runs an Apprenticeships advertising campaign bi-annually: in advance of Northern Ireland Apprenticeship Week in February; and during August for the GCSE / A level results timeframe.   Our media campaign has been updated in 2024 and will revitalise our audience engagement to ensure messaging is meaningful, clearly understood and connects with the diverse range of interested groups, from students and their parents, all age jobseekers, to HR professionals and local businesses. A review is currently underway of all available DfE supporting materials for employers.  Finally, my Department is developing a three year Apprenticeship Action Plan to help expand the pipeline of future talent for business.  This includes an Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund to increase the diversity of those who undertake and benefit from apprenticeship provision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any progress made on producing an Action Plan for Lifelong Learning, as set out in the Skills Strategy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials have been working closely with the Alliance for Lifelong Learning, with a view to exploring and co-designing effective approaches to creating a culture of lifelong learning here. Via my Skills Fund, we are pursuing several initiatives which will enhance awareness of adult learning opportunities and demonstrate and promote the impact and benefits of participation in adult learning. The first of these initiatives – research into impact measurement of adult learning, especially non-accredited learning – is now about to commence, led by the Alliance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for a breakdown of the total running costs for each Invest NI office around the world in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Invest NI’s international offices are categorised into four overseas territories, expenditure incurred is reported by each territory. In the last five financial years, the running costs (comprising staff and office related costs such as rents, utilities and telecoms, to the nearest thousand) reported were:Financial year2019-202020-212021-222022-232023-24Territory£’000£’000£’000£’000£’000Asia Pacific9261,0091,0021,5591,510India, Middle East & Africa1,0471,0009611,2681,105GB & Europe2,6162,4731,9862,0332,836Americas2,8353,4662,7262,8053,193

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will commission research to develop an evaluation of the economic value of the night-time economy (i) in Belfast; and (ii) across Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I recognise the important contribution of the night-time economy to ensuring our towns and cities are places where people want to live, work, invest and visit.  There is insufficient empirical evidence on our night-time economy at present and my Department is in the early phases of commissioning research to understand its value and potential. I anticipate that I will be able to provide a positive update on this research in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide an organisational chart of his Department that includes the various (i) advisory boards; (ii) advisory councils; (iii) task forces; (iv) expert panels; and (v) similar bodies that have been established in his Department since he took up office.
answer_text: The Department for the Economy organisation chart can be found here:Department for the Economy Organisation ChartThe bodies listed below have been set up since I became Minister for Economy.  The divisions responsible, which can be found in the organisation chart, are listed beside them.Business and Employment Regulation Division - Credit Union Working Group Higher Education Division - Magee Expansion Taskforce Business, Gas, Minerals and Renewable Electricity Division – Gas Network Strategic Steering Group Gas Network Strategic Working GroupHydrogen Consultation Working Group Northern Ireland Hydrogen Industry Working Group Biomethane Production Policy Working Group 80 by 30 Group Tourism and Telecoms Division - Tourism Partnership Board

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the new (i) advisory boards; (ii) advisory councils; (iii) task forces; (iv) expert panels; and (v) similar bodies that have been established in his Department since he took up office.
answer_text: Since I took up office in February 2024, the following have been established:Credit Union Working Group - through a co-design approach, aims to identify legislative and policy changes necessary to deliver the Irish League of Credit Unions 2024 policy manifesto.Magee Expansion Taskforce - established to develop an action plan with associated timescales and approximate costings to achieve 10,000 students at the Derry-Londonderry Campus of Ulster University.Gas Network Strategic Steering Group - a strategic group with the objective of evolving the gas network towards a net zero future.Gas Network Strategic Working Group - the operational sub group of the above group to manage the current price control regime and prepare for future price controls/operational work to evolve the gas network and integrate affordable and sustainable green gas.Hydrogen Consultation Working Group – aims to provide input to developing a clean hydrogen policy for Northern Ireland.Northern Ireland Hydrogen Industry Working Group - aims to inform policy makers on the barriers and issues they face to growth.Biomethane Production Policy Working Group – aims to co-design policy for biomethane production in NI with DAERA and the NI Utility. 80 by 30 Group – aims to investigate options to ensure the Climate Change Act 2022 target “that at least 80% of electricity consumption is from renewable sources by 2030” is met. Tourism Partnership Board - to advise and make recommendations in support of the development and delivery of a Tourism Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how the Independent Review of Invest Northern Ireland Action Plan will be implemented to deliver improved performance and effectiveness, particularly in the context of its role in delivering the Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: The Invest NI Review Action Plan was developed to deliver the profound change called for by the Independent Review Panel. Implementation of the Action Plan is progressing well with 17 actions already completed, including the introduction of new leadership.		Invest NI will play a key role in delivering my plan for regional balance and Promoting Places and Partnerships is one of the strategic priorities set out in the organisation’s draft Business Strategy 2024-27.		I have tasked Invest NI with delivering 65% of its investments outside the Belfast Metropolitan Area by the end of the Strategy period. To support this, Invest NI will increase its capacity and presence across the North, with staff resources deployed in the regions increasing by 40%, and the Regional Offices also leading on the delivery of a new Regional Property Programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of (i) whether Belfast is an economic driver for the whole of Northern Ireland; and (ii) how this can complement the Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: Belfast is a key driver of economic growth, contributing over 30% to the North’s GVA.   It therefore has an important role to play in supporting and sustaining businesses and supply chains across  the North. Belfast will continue to be a key driver of economic growth and it is important that we can maximise the diffusion of these benefits throughout the North. Increased collaboration and partnership will be key to make this to happen. The Sub Regional Plan which encourages collaboration across geographic areas can provide a framework for this.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how projects that contribute to net zero will be prioritised for funding as part of the Local Economic Partnerships model.
answer_text: Projects will be brought forward by the Local Economic Partnerships (LEPs), who are best placed to identify local needs. The Department and Invest NI will work alongside LEPs to ensure that proposed actions align with my four key priorities, including net zero.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the work of the Northern Ireland Export Forum in developing an action plan to achieve economic transformation through maximising external sales.
answer_text: The Export Forum was established in April 2023 and meets on a regular basis. It brings together the main providers of export advice services in the North – those working directly with the small businesses and entrepreneurs who represent the backbone of our economy. An important objective for the Forum is to facilitate greater collaboration and communication between service providers, for the benefit of businesses. Good progress is being made in that regard. The Forum is also engaged in the outworking of the strategic actions within the Independent Review of Invest NI Action Plan, as they relate to internationalisation and export support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of children that received a statement of special educational needs due to allergies in the last 12 months.
answer_text: Neither the department nor the Education Authority (EA) hold this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans his Department has for the future use of the Muckamore Abbey Hospital site.
answer_text: As the owner of the Muckamore Abbey Hospital site, the Belfast Trust is responsible for planning and implementing the closure arrangements.  As part of these arrangements the Trust recently published the results of a public consultation on an Equality Impact Assessment in relation to the decommissioning and closure of the Muckamore Abbey Hospital site.  The report can be accessed on their website at Consultation Outcomes | Belfast Health & Social Care Trust website (hscni.net). The Belfast Trust have confirmed to my Department that they do not expect to have any further use for the site when the remaining patients are successfully resettled and the hospital closes.  My Department is currently carrying out an assessment to establish whether there is any regional strategic Health and Social Care interest in the site, and the outcome of this will inform decisions about the future use of the site.  My officials will continue to liaise with the Belfast Trust on  work to close the hospital, including the outcome of this assessment once completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on progress made by the Northern Ireland Export Forum in relation to setting ambitious targets for 2030 exports.
answer_text: The Export Forum has met regularly since its establishment in April 2023. Recognising the virtuous circle between exporting, innovation and productivity, one of the Forum’s first actions was to agree a stretching ambition to grow external sales intensity  (total sales outside of the North as a percentage of Gross Domestic Product) to 60% by 2030, from a baseline of 45% in 2019. Achieving this would place the North amongst the world’s highest performing small advanced economies.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of road traffic accidents in the last two years that have involved someone who had failed to inform the DVA or DVLA that they had been deemed medically unfit to drive.
answer_text: The Driver & Vehicle Agency (DVA) is responsible for the administration of driver licensing in the north and the Driver & Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) performs this function for drivers who are resident in Britain.The Department does not hold the information you have requested in respect of road traffic accidents involving someone who had failed to inform the DVA that they had been deemed medically unfit to drive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 13929/22-27.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of occasions in the last 12 months that doctors or other medical professionals have disclosed information to the Driver and Vehicle Agency about a patients' lack of fitness to drive.
answer_text: Whilst the Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) holds the information requested, it is not held in a readily accessible format. Around 16,000 cases were referred to the DVA’s medical advisory service for medical assessment in the last 12 months.  To identify those cases where doctors or other medical professionals have disclosed information about a patient’s lack of fitness to drive, would require the DVA to examine each record.  This information could only be obtained at a disproportionate cost.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the work being undertaken to develop the Three-year Forward Look Action Plan for skills.
answer_text: My officials are considering the recommendations of the Northern Ireland Audit Office Report – Developing Skills for Northern Ireland’s Future – before finalising advice on the Skills Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 11245/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 11245 22-27 was answered on 8 November 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether holding a driving licence has become a requirement for gaining employment as a midwife.
answer_text: Rotational or community midwifery roles do require a driving licence to meet the full needs of the post.  However, this requirement will be waived for any applicant whose disability prohibits driving but has access to a form of transport approved by the Trust, which permits them to carry out the duties of the post.Any post-specific flexibilities or exceptions will be stated in recruitment advertisements and job specifications issued by Trusts.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any consideration he has given to placing an obligation on GPs and other medical professionals to inform the DVLA when they have deemed an individual medically unfit to drive.
answer_text: The Department for Infrastructure has a statutory duty to be satisfied as to the medical fitness to drive of an applicant for, or holder of, a driving licence, under the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.  Standards and guidance for medical professionals in relation to medical fitness to drive are contained in the publication: Assessing fitness to drive: a guide for medical professionals - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) which is issued by the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency. Doctors and other healthcare professionals have a duty to follow all relevant professional standards and guidelines, including those referred to within the DVLA guidance relating to fitness to drive. The relevant professional standards for doctors are set by the General Medical Council which are available at:  Confidentiality: patients' fitness to drive and reporting concerns to the DVLA or DVA - professional standards - GMC (gmc-uk.org)

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, in relation to live cases with the Troubles Permanent Disablement Payment Scheme, to detail how many days from initial application that the longest running case has been awaiting a decision.
answer_text: The progress of each application is dependent on the provision of supporting information from external organisations, which is outside of the control of the Victims’ Payments Board (VPB).  Applications progress through the various stages of the Scheme at different rates. Given the historical nature of the subject matter, the passage of time makes certain records difficult or impossible to retrieve.  The VPB are often dealing with incidents that happened up to 50 years ago.  It is also important to highlight that approximately one in three applications have multiple incidents with varying levels of detail to set against the tests required by the legislation that underpin the Scheme.  Each incident requires both verification of eligibility and evidence and requires a separate determination, which can take a considerable amount of time to process. Delays also occur where an application form is incomplete, supporting evidence is missing or there are gaps in incident disclosure etc. Application processing includes but is not limited to; verification checks, evidence gathering, and a medical assessment.  The average processing time may be impacted by the provision of medical information from GPs and Health Trusts which enable the completion of a medical assessment (carried out by Capita).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any engagement he has had with Belfast City Council through the Historic Environment Division in relation to the restoration of Wilmont House in South Belfast.
answer_text: I have had no such engagement.		Officials within my Historic Environment Division - HED - have been engaged with Belfast City Council since February 2017, when they asked for access to Wilmont House.  		Later in 2017 HED – having surveyed the building - advised the council that this listed building was in a very poor condition and might be suitable for an Urgent Works Notice.		HED asked the Council in 2019 who should be contacted regarding ongoing deterioration and water ingress, and for any updates on maintenance activities to address dilapidation. The Council advised that a contractor had been engaged to disconnect the water supply.		HED then met officials within the Council’s asset team in 2022 to advise of necessary repairs and vegetation clearance.  		A further onsite meeting took place between HED and BCC officials in October 2022 to discuss the works needed to save the house from further dilapidation. HED stressed that works were required urgently to clear gutters and vegetation and also advised the council to get advice from a conservation accredited structural engineer.		In 2023 HED sent a copy of the ‘Protocol for the Care of the Government Historic Estate’ document to the Council (this was commended to Councils as good practice by the NI Executive in 2011). The Council agreed to commission an independent evaluation of the options to restore Wilmont House back into use, however HED understands a feasibility study has yet to be undertaken. HED officials retain a watching brief on this building.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the mechanisms in place to enforce the removal of individuals from driving who have been deemed medically unfit to drive; and (ii) whether he is minded to enhance enforcement.
answer_text: Where licence holders inform the Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) of medical conditions likely to impair safe driving, the DVA conducts medical investigations to determine whether the person should be permitted to drive.  In normal circumstances, the person may continue to drive pending conclusion of these medical investigations, except where they have been advised not to do so by their doctors.  Drivers are also warned of the legal consequences of failing to report the onset or deterioration of medical conditions which are likely to affect their ability to drive safely. Where a person is deemed not to meet the strict standards of medical fitness to drive, the DVA will issue a notice revoking the person’s licence or informing them of the decision to refuse to grant a licence.  The notice will inform the person of their right of appeal against the decision and set out the legal requirement for the person to surrender any licence held by them without delay.  Where a licence is not surrendered in a timely manner, and without reasonable excuse, the DVA will request the PSNI to seize the licence.The PSNI has primary responsibility for the enforcement of road traffic offences, including offences relating to driving licences.  The PSNI has access to the DVA’s Driver Licensing System, which includes information on whether a person holds a valid licence, has had a licence revoked or has been disqualified from holding or obtaining a licence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on progress to implement the Screen Industries Sectoral Action Plan.
answer_text: The Screen Industries Sectoral Action Plan is progressing well. A key milestone will be the opening of Studio Ulster, a cutting-edge virtual production facility, in early 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the (i) membership; and (ii) terms of reference for the newly-established Canadian Advisory Council.
answer_text: The table below lists the members of the newly established Canadian Advisory Council. It is comprised of C-suite level executives from sectors including Agri-Tech, Advanced Manufacturing & Engineering, and Fintech & financial services. The Council members are engaged in a voluntary capacity based on their connection to the North of Ireland and their willingness to support the growth of the North’s Economy.Rodney HillDermot MuirTerence DobbinGary McDowellJohn SpeersJenny O'DonnellJohn LaughlinStephen McCulloughMairead LaveryJohn GalwayBrenda GardinerIan CampbellKevin Magee	Its terms of reference is to support Invest Northern Ireland’s trade and FDI strategy. The Advisory Council will help local businesses expand their presence in the Canadian market and seek to open a channel for Canadian companies interested in investing in the north of Ireland.	The Council will operate for an initial period of 2 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the implementation of the Fintech and Financial Services Sectoral Action Plan.
answer_text: The Fintech and Financial Services and Software (including Cyber) action plans were developed in conjunction and contain complementary actions which will help support the growth of the Fintech and Financial Services sector. A number of actions are already underway, including in relation to skills, which I recognise as a key issue. I recently launched the Digital Skills Action Plan to support and develop the key skills required by innovative sectors such as Fintech. Along with delivery partners, including InvestNI, the Department is exploring ways to help develop the voice of the sector and to promote all-island opportunities, including the emerging Fintech Corridor.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans he has to develop or dispose of the Forster Green Hospital site in South Belfast.
answer_text: Forster Green remains a strategically important site for the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust and the Trust continues to deliver a number of services from this site.  The Trust currently has no plans to develop or dispose of this site.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on efforts to increase afforestation.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any consideration he has given to making the completion of a small and micro business impact test a statutory requirement across all government departments and arm's-length bodies.
answer_text: The small and micro business impact test (SAMBIT) is an important part of our policymaking process and an integral part of the Regulatory Impact Assessment (RIA) toolkit.In line with Executive guidance, I, and fellow Ministers, require an RIA to be considered and, if not screened out, to be applied and used to support policy development for any new, or amendments to existing, policy proposals.I have asked my officials to work closely with the Federation of Small Businesses (FSB) to identify how the profile of the SAMBIT can be further built and developed in order to better champion its consideration and usage as an integral part of the policymaking process.To make the SAMBIT a specific statutory requirement would require significant legislative underpinning. The current legislative programme is already under significant pressure; as such, there are no plans to consider this in the current mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the economic contribution of childminders.
answer_text: In my economic vision I set out the need to invest in affordable childcare and fair pay for childcare workers because I recognise the contribution of this sector to the local economy. Registered childminders provide parents with a flexible childcare option allowing them to enter and remain in employment or pursue educational opportunities. This support is essential for maintaining parental workforce participation therefore stimulating economic activity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he plans to update the Highway Code.
answer_text: I am aware that in Britain a substantive update to the Highway Code was made in 2022. The most significant change was the introduction of Hierarchy of Users. The intended objective of the Hierarchy was to enhance the safety of those considered to be the most vulnerable road users. At the time, I understand that the Department for Transport undertook a very substantial publicity campaign, at a cost of more that £2million, to promote public awareness of the changes. Statistics are not yet available to demonstrate whether the changes have been effective in reducing the number of people killed or seriously injured. Locally, I will consider any initiatives that can improve road safety. However, taking a similar approach here would require significant research and development, stakeholder engagement and public consultation. Thereafter, in the event that a hierarchy of road users was to be adopted, a significant ring-fenced budget would be required to deliver an effective public information campaign.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Digital Skills Action Plan 2024-2034.
answer_text: The Digital Skills Action Plan will launch on Wednesday 25th September 2024. The plan was developed in conjunction with industry and educational experts and will help us deliver the digital workforce needed to thrive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the progress to implement the Life and Health Sciences Sectoral Action Plan.
answer_text: My Department, along with key delivery partners, have been working on wide range of activities that will help deliver the actions within the Life and Health Science (LHS) Sectoral Action Plan over the next 3 years. Actions being supported include investing in skills and labour which have a significant impact on productivity and innovation. Also, greater investment in R&D and in innovative, cutting-edge technologies and methods to improve efficiency.  A key action from the LHS Sectoral Action Plan, developing the Future Medicines Institute, (FMI) is underway and the Project Initiation date of 1 January 2025 is on track. The FMI Project is an example of excellent collaboration across key stakeholders including, DfE, Invest NI, Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, UK Research and Innovation and Academia.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the progress to implement the Advanced Manufacturing, Materials and Engineering Sectoral Action Plan.
answer_text: Work continues with key delivery partners to implement the 25 key actions within the Advanced Manufacturing, Materials and Engineering (AMME) sectoral action plan to help grow the sector and deliver on my key economic priorities. Actions being supported includes investing in skills and labour which have a significant impact on productivity, innovation, and supply chain. Also, greater investment in R&D and in innovative, cutting-edge technologies and methods to improve efficiency through the City and Growth Deal investments which provide access to automation, smart design, digital twinning, and artificial intelligence.  As part of the Action Plan, Invest NI offer a range of assistance to enhance the sustainability and competitiveness of local operations and production lines through initiatives such as Grant for R&D support, Innovation Vouchers, Energy Efficiency Capital Grant, and the Resource Efficiency Capital Grant.   InterTradeIreland, another key delivery partner, are also supporting cross border exports and business capability including a collaborative cross border skills project between Mid-Ulster MEGA (North) and Savvy Teens (South) which sees secondary schools from both the North and South working alongside manufacturing and engineering companies across the border.  In addition, engagement is ongoing with lead officials both within and outside the Department to consider opportunities for the AMME sector within an Enhanced Investment Zone for the North of Ireland to leverage the North’s opportunities in the global transition to a green and sustainable future. Collaboration with delivery bodies including the sector’s advisory body, the Makers Alliance, will continue including discussions around a proposal to build a super cluster to grow the AMME sector as part of a wider DfE cluster policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will publish the public consultation on (i) adult to child ratios; and (ii) health declarations for prospective childcare workers, as part of the broader review of Minimum Standards for Childminding and Day Care.
answer_text: The first stage of the review of the Minimum Standards, a public consultation on adult-child ratios and the requirement for all prospective childcare workers to provide a health declaration countersigned by their GP, was launched on Monday 24 March 2025.Following the close of the consultation on 20 June 2025, all responses will be collated for review by the Department, and a consultation report will be produced. If it is considered that any urgent changes should be made to the Minimum Standards in advance of the completion of the wider review, these changes will be communicated through a Departmental Press Release, a Circular to HSC Trusts and a letter to all childcare providers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) how much of the £25 million allocated to childcare has been spent to this point; and (ii) for his assessment of how much of it will be spent by the end of the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: The full £25m early learning and childcare funding has been allocated to a range of DE funded programmes and wider delivery partners, including other departments. My officials are working collaboratively with them to help ensure the full allocation is spent by the end of March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the work of the (i) Task and Finish Group; and (ii) Review Reference Group established to support the review of the Minimum Standards for Childminding and Day Care.
answer_text: The first stage of the review of the Minimum Standards, a public consultation on adult-child ratios and the requirement for all prospective childcare workers to provide a health declaration countersigned by their GP, was launched on Monday 24 March 2025.Following the close of the consultation on 20 June 2025, all responses will be collated for review by the Department, and a consultation report will be produced.  The Review Reference Group will also be established following the close of the consultation to consider the wider Minimum Standards in detail and assist with the drafting of a redesigned set of Standards for consultation early in 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 9445/22-27, to detail any consideration he has given to reviewing the need to have annual inspections for registered childminders.
answer_text: The requirement for registered childcare provision to be inspected on an annual basis is contained within the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995, and is considered to be an important measure in ensuring that children and families have access to high quality, safe care. That said, as part of the ongoing review of the Minimum Standards for Childminding and Day Care, I have asked my officials to seek input from stakeholders on inspection frequency.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any engagement they have had with the Belfast night tsar in relation to the implementation of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls.
answer_text: The Delivery Plan includes an action to develop, through sectoral working groups, agreed EVAWG action plans including for Safer Socialising (Action 8). The Safer Socialising Steering Group, which is chaired by Hospitality Ulster, contains representatives from a range of organisations including officials from TEO, other Departments, PSNI, and Translink.The Belfast Night Czar, Michael Stewart, recently made an introductory presentation on his role to the Safer Socialising Steering Group. Our officials will continue to engage with the Night Czar through this group.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with the Minister of Education in relation to enhancing employment rights for substitute teachers, including the provision of holiday and sick pay.
answer_text: Policy responsibility for teachers’ pay and conditions rests with the Department of Education and there is no role for my Department. However, I have launched a consultation on a Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill which closes on 30th September 2024 and would encourage teachers and their representatives to contribute to the consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any consultation he has undertaken with the Minister of Education in relation to the employment rights of substitute teachers as part of the development of the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: In June 2024, I wrote to all Ministerial colleagues to advise of the launch of the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill Consultation.  The consultation is open until 30th September 2024.   I encourage everyone, including those who have sector specific concerns about how proposals may affect them, or their industry, to respond to the consultation.Terms and conditions of employment for substitute teachers are a matter for the employing authorities and the Department of Education.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with the First Minister and deputy First Minister in relation to the implementation of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls in further and higher education settings.
answer_text: While I have not had any meetings with the First Minister and deputy First Minister specifically in relation to the implementation of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls in further and higher education settings, a senior official has represented my Department on the Oversight Board.  I welcome the framework and am committed to working across government and with all stakeholders, including student representatives, in contributing to its implementation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with student unions in relation to the implementation of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls in further and higher education settings.
answer_text: Officials from my Department meet regularly with NUS-USI representatives in order to discuss a range of issues important to students. I welcome the framework and am committed to working across government and with all stakeholders, including student representatives, in contributing to its implementation.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an assessment of how their Department has managed the functions of the Northern Ireland Strategic Migration Partnership that are under their remit.
To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of how the United Kingdom's withdrawal from the European Union has impacted the six further education colleges.
answer_text: My officials have engaged with the Further Education Colleges and have been advised that while Britains withdrawal from the European Union has had an impact on their engagement with international exchange programmes, cross border collaboration and the recruitment of international students, the extent of this impact has not been analysed. A range of factors will have had a contributory effect to these activities, therefore, other than reporting that the Colleges have received less funding for the activities identified under the Turing Scheme post EU Exit in comparison to its predecessor Erasmus+ pre EU Exit, I am unable to provide further information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his plans to fund additional initial teacher training places in the Irish medium.
answer_text: The Department for Education is responsible for determining the number of teachers to be trained each year across the primary, post-primary and Irish medium sectors.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when he will approve a final version of the tourism strategy.
answer_text: I have established a Tourism Partnership Board whose core purpose is to develop and oversee a final Tourism Action Plan that will be co-designed and co-delivered with the industry. The first meeting of the Board took place on 11 September 2024 with further meetings scheduled in October and November. It is anticipated the final Action Plan will be published by the end of the year.The Tourism Action Plan is also being informed by analysis of the responses to a public consultation undertaken by my Department at the beginning of this year. This analysis was published on 12 September 2024 and is available on the departmental website.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will create a green investment unit for Invest NI to look at sustainability across all Invest NI projects and to attract green economy investments.
answer_text: My Department’s Low Carbon/ Net Zero Sectoral Action plan sets out objectives including promoting the Green Economy in the North of Ireland and supporting North of Ireland businesses to optimise strategic opportunities emerging from the global drive to net zero. Invest NI is a key delivery partner in the implementation of this action plan. Invest NI currently has two teams, one focussed on green investment, the other on sustainability:The Green Economy Development Team focuses on identifying and promoting diversification opportunities into new and emerging green markets/supply chains; positioning the North’s capability on the global stage to support the Invest NI's trade and investment functions; and advising on strategic projects. The Energy Resource and Efficiency Team offers a suite of support including technical advice, sustainability planning and support for capital investments. This enables businesses to achieve cost, carbon, material and energy savings.In response to the Independent Review of Invest Northern Ireland, my Department and Invest NI have set several strategic actions to further strengthen focus on the Green Economy. This includes creating a dedicated Green Economy Division and embedding Net Zero and Green Economy targets into Invest NI’s Business Plan, governance, and delivery models.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of properties operated by the Housing Executive that are designated as pet friendly.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive does not have a ‘pet-friendly’ classification for its own stock. This is because all tenants can apply in writing to the relevant Area Manager to seek their consent to keep a domestic pet. General Conditions of tenancy clauses (9,10 - Part 1 Tenant’s Obligations) regarding pet ownership apply to all our domestic dwellings, i.e:Pets9. Not to keep any domestic pets without the consent in writing of the Area Manager (if the dwelling is a flat or maisonette). 10. Not to keep more than one domestic pet without the consent in writing of the Area Manager (if the dwelling is not a flat or maisonette)”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the progress that has been made on the delivery of the Skills Strategy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The economic vision I set out in February is now featured in the draft Programme for Government. It focuses on good jobs, increasing productivity, improving regional balance and reducing carbon emissions. I provided an update to the Assembly on my priorities for skills in July, including the creation of a Ring-fenced Skills Fund which will maintain and further develop initiatives that support participation in lifelong learning and skills to drive innovation and productivity. My broader programme of investment in skills will prioritise the expansion of education provision to address skills imbalances across the North. Significant good work has been delivered under the auspices of the Skills Strategy and its goals and objectives remain central to my skills agenda. However, it is essential that we now recalibrate the delivery programme to align with the refreshed economic vision.  Officials are working on a three-year action plan for skills which will set out full details on the way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether any of the planned future social housing delivered by the Housing Executive will be designated as pet friendly.
answer_text: Any planned future social housing, whether it is owned by the Housing Executive or by Housing Associations, will be subject to those organisations policies on pet ownership. Therefore, whether a tenant is permitted to keep a pet in their home now or in the future is an operational matter for the Housing Executive and Housing Associations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he has any plans to introduce a pilot for default 20mph speed limits.
answer_text: The Department’s approach to setting speed limits is set out in Policy and Procedure Guide RsPPG E051 ‘Setting local speed limits in Northern Ireland’ and a brief outline of the procedures is on NIDirect: Speed limits - how they are set | nidirect. There are currently no plans to introduce a pilot for default 20 mph speed limits, however, the Department’s approach to speed limits in general is one of the topics that I have been discussing with officials during my time in office. One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any actions he will take to improve road safety on the A1 between Dromore and Newry, including the (i) enhancement of white line markings; and (ii) replacement of non-functioning road reflecting studs.
answer_text: The A1 Junctions Phase 2 Road Improvement Scheme, which aims to provide further safety improvements along the A1 dual carriageway between Hillsborough and Loughbrickland, is currently moving forward to the procurement process. The proposals include the provision of 4 new grade separated junctions, the closing up of all gaps in the central reservation and the provision of a continuous central reserve safety barrier along the whole route. Other significant improvements are also included.I recently made an announcement in the Assembly on 2 July 2024 stating that the scheme will now progress to the final delivery stage as a single package of works with ringfenced funding going forward.Preparation for the procurement process is moving forward at pace, involving the finalisation of the contract documents, in readiness for a formal procurement competition in 2025. The documents are substantially complete.Construction of the whole scheme is expected to take three years resulting in an opening date in Spring/Summer 2029.The full proposals can be viewed via the following link: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/a1-junctions-phase-2-overviewThe road markings and studs are inspected on an annual basis. The most recent inspection is from September 2024.  A programme of repairs is being arranged and will be completed by end of October, subject to favourable weather.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an assessment of the longevity of pothole repairs carried out by his Department.
answer_text: The longevity of pothole repairs can be impacted by weather conditions, particularly heavy rainfall or very low temperatures, as well as the quality of the adjacent road surface. Additionally, there may be an operational need to carry out a temporary repair at certain sites to remove the defect until arrangements are made for a permanent repair. In general, however, the minimum expected longevity of a repair would be two years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the number of potholes filled by his Department at the junction of Gallywally Avenue and Saintfield Road, South Belfast, in the last 12 months; and (ii) his assessment of the longevity of the repairs carried out at this location.
answer_text: My officials have advised that in the last 12 months 33 actionable carriageway defects have been noted along the A24 Ormeau Road /Saintfield Road from the roundabout at the Ravenhill Road/Ormeau Road junction to the junction with the A55 Upper Knockbreda Road. All 33 carriageway defects have been repaired.  The longevity of pothole repairs largely depends on the repair method and materials used.  Temporary fixes like cold patching may have less durability, especially in areas with heavy traffic or when repaired during inclement weather.  Permanent repairs, using hot mix asphalt can extend the lifespan of the repair to several years, offering a more durable solution.  My Department’s aim is to carry out first time repairs using hot mix asphalt to provide a permanent repair and avoid unnecessary and repeat visits to the one location. All permanent repairs retain a one-year warranty period under the terms of the contract. Resurfacing schemes are funded through my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme; regrettably, for some time the available investment has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain all our roads in a reasonable condition.  While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need of intervention.  A resurfacing scheme on the A24 Ormeau Road/Saintfield Road (between its junction with the Ormeau Road/Ravenhill Road roundabout and Drumkeen Manor) has been identified for inclusion in the 2025/26 resurfacing programme, subject to the availability of the necessary financial resource.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the Housing Executive's policy on tenants owning and keeping pets in their properties.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“The Housing Executive’s position on pets is set out in clauses 9 and 10 of Part 1 (Tenant’s Obligations) of the General Conditions of Tenancy. Upon signing a tenancy agreement and accepting these conditions, our tenants agree: ‘9. Not to keep any domestic pets without the consent in writing of the Area Manager (if the dwelling is a flat or maisonette). 10. Not to keep more than one domestic pet without the consent in writing of the Area Manager (if the dwelling is not a flat or maisonette).’Coupled with these specific conditions relating to ownership of pets, responsible ownership of the same is provided for by clause 5 of Part 1 (Tenant’s Obligations) whereby tenants agree ‘not to do or permit or suffer to be done in the dwelling or within the curtilage or neighbourhood of the dwelling any act or thing which is or may be an annoyance or nuisance to the occupiers of any neighbouring or adjoining premises’. If our tenants observe the above obligations, they may quietly enjoy their dwelling, and keep a pet/pets without any interruption by the Housing Executive.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with the Executive Office in relation to the role of Invest NI as part of the development of an updated Executive International Relations Strategy.
answer_text: My department engages as appropriate with the Executive Office on areas of mutual interest, including how and where we showcase our capabilities to an international audience.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any plans he has to develop modelling and an informed assessment of the benefits of the provision of paid carer’s leave.
answer_text: The Good Jobs consultation covers a wide range of potential employment rights changes, including the introduction of a new Carer’s Leave entitlement. As part of this consultation respondents are asked for their views on whether this entitlement should be a paid or unpaid right. Responses from the consultation will help provide an informed assessment of potential benefits of paid carer’s leave.There are many variables to be considered in order to determine the true potential cost of paid carer’s leave to the Executive and assess the benefits. Research, including the consideration of relevant publications and the provision of carer’s leave in other jurisdictions, is ongoing. Engagement with key stakeholders, including CarersNI and a cross departmental working group on issues impacting carers, is also ongoing.	In addition, further modelling and refinement of the potentuial cost of a paid entitlement is being undertaken.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with HMRC in relation to the potential removal of worker as a form of employment status.
answer_text: My officials will liaise with British Government officials as any proposals develop.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with HMRC in relation to facilitating the potential payment of statutory paid carer's leave.
answer_text: HMRC have been asked by departmental officials to impact the potential cost of making the necessary system changes to facilitate a statutory payment related to carer’s leave, and engagement meetings are scheduled to take place in relation to this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the Executive’s public finances Sustainability Plan, as referenced in the Interim Fiscal Framework.
answer_text: My officials have been working intensively over the summer and the Budget Sustainability Plan is now approaching completion. Whilst it was initially due to be published by the end of August, the inability to engage with Treasury during the pre-election period meant that I was able to secure a short extension to the deadline with the Chief Secretary to the Treasury and I intend to bring it to the Executive for their agreement in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the engagement they have had with the Minister for the Economy on the implementation of the High Street Task Force report on Delivering a 21st Century High Street.
answer_text: The scope of the High Street Taskforce recommendations span the responsibilities of a number of departments and we are of the view that they should be taken forward by a cross-departmental group of the relevant departments, along with local councils. Therefore, we wrote to Ministerial colleagues at the Department for Communities, Department for the Economy, Department of Finance and Department for Infrastructure on 10th October 2024, asking them to consider the recommendations and determine the way forward, bringing matters to the Executive for consideration as necessary.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of meetings of the High Street Task Force that have taken place in the last 12 months.
answer_text: The High Street Task Force presented their Report to Junior Ministers in March 2022.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when they will finalise their consideration of the recent submission from officials in relation to the High Street Task Force report on Delivering a 21st Century High Street and the implementation of its findings.
answer_text: The scope of the High Street Taskforce recommendations span the responsibilities of a number of departments and we are of the view that they should be taken forward by a cross-departmental group of the relevant departments, along with local councils. Therefore, we wrote to Ministerial colleagues at the Department for Communities, Department for the Economy, Department of Finance and Department for Infrastructure on 10th October 2024, asking them to consider the recommendations and determine the way forward, bringing matters to the Executive for consideration as necessary.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the operation of the Northern Ireland Concessionary Fares Scheme for Asylum Seekers.
answer_text: Asylum seekers who meet the eligibility requirements for the NI Concessionary Fares Scheme on the grounds of age or disability can access concessionary travel. In June 2023, my Department launched a public consultation on changes to eligibility for the Scheme, which included an option to extend concessionary fares to all asylum seekers. However, due to funding pressures, I will not be taking forward any of the options which would increase the costs of the Scheme, at this time.I will however remove the three-month residency condition and extend the list of accepted documentation to prove residency. This will help make the Scheme more accessible to those who are already eligible, including asylum seekers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any plans he has to develop a dedicated hospitality strategy and action plan.
answer_text: I recognise the contribution of the hospitality sector to both our tourism offer and the wider economy.		I have established a Tourism Partnership Board to support my Department in the co-design and development of a Tourism Action Plan to lay out a vision for the sustainable and inclusive growth of our valuable tourism sector. To ensure a collaborative co-design approach, membership of the Partnership Board consists of representatives from both the tourism industry and the hospitality sector, alongside Executive Departments and local government. 		This will ensure that issues important to the hospitality sector will be considered, such as the need for a bespoke Skills Action Plan to address skills gaps, rewarding careers including competitive salaries, career pathways and equitable distribution of opportunities throughout the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his plans to implement recommendation 28 of the Skills for a 10x Economy - Skills Strategy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials have been engaging with the Alliance for Lifelong Learning to consider how best my Department can work with partners in support of our ambition to create a culture of lifelong learning here. We are pursuing several potential initiatives via the Skills Fund, with a view to enhancing awareness of adult learning opportunities, along with demonstrating and promoting the impact and benefits of participation in adult learning.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any plans he has to develop a standalone manufacturing strategy.
answer_text: The Department’s recently launched Action Plan to grow the Advanced Manufacturing, Materials and Engineering (AMME) sector outlines proposed actions that will also help grow the manufacturing sector and address challenges facing the sector. Central to the work undertaken to develop this Action Plan has been a period of extensive collaboration with industry through engagement with the sector’s main advisory body, the Makers Alliance.  Makers Alliance was formed in 2021 to be the overarching independent advisory body for manufacturing, supported by Manufacturing NI, QUB and UU and funded by Invest NI.      Since its creation, the industry-led Makers Alliance has delivered several substantive pieces of work, focused on ‘one plan, one voice, one vision’ for manufacturing in the North of Ireland.  These include: a Strategic Plan for Manufacturing in the North ‘Making a Better Future’ and a Delivery Plan for Manufacturing in the North ‘Vision with Action’. The Department’s Action Plan is in alignment with both key documents, thereby I have no plans to develop a standalone manufacturing strategy.The proposed actions within the Department’s Action Plan support investment in skills and R&D, in emerging and sustainable technologies for future growth, in grasping the opportunities to grow and prosper. Through the Action Plan, the Department and key delivery partners such as Invest NI, InterTradeIreland and the Makers Alliance network which includes Manufacturing NI will continue to work with the manufacturing sector to harness its potential.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the criteria used by the Education Authority to assess whether a child is suitable to undergo statutory assessment; and (ii) the engagement the Education Authority has with medical professionals involved in caring for the child as part of this process.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised me of the following:When a request or referral for a statutory assessment is received, the EA has a duty to enter into a consideration period.  To inform the consideration of the referral or request, the EA notifies potential advice givers (child’s school, Educational Psychology Service, Health Trusts and/or a pupil support service) and parents and asks for any relevant information to inform the decision as to whether a Statutory Assessment is required. The EA will consider the child’s presentation of need as described by parents and those involved, what supports/reasonable and purposeful measures have been provided at the school-based stages of the code of practice and how the child has responded to these supports.  Where it is clear that the child’s needs are significant and complex and not responding to appropriate measures and that all available supports have been exhausted, the EA will likely consider that a statutory assessment is warranted.  There will of course be circumstances in which the child’s needs are so significant, severe and/or profound and it is evident that provision additional to and otherwise different to what a mainstream school can offer beyond their current resources means that a decision will be made to progress the statutory assessment.  Each child’s circumstances are considered individually. Community Paediatricians and Social Services are statutory advice givers.Once a statutory assessment is progressed, the HSCT is requested to provide statutory advice in relation to the child’s medical presentation/condition and the impact that any associated special educational needs (SEN) may have on their access to the curriculum. Advice may be provided by the Allied Health Professionals (Speech & Language Therapy, Occupational Therapy, Physiotherapy), Nursing and CAMHS as well as Community Paediatricians and social services. Health staff, where necessary, will also contribute to the review of a child’s statement and provide updated reports. They will also, where necessary, contribute to multi-disciplinary meetings to support understanding of a child’s needs and the provision that may be required.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to provide an update on the implementation of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: Question 10 - Kate NichollTo ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to provide an update on the implementation of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.AQO 760/22-27Answer:The first requirement of the Act has been met through the making and bringing into operation of the Climate Change (Reporting Bodies) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2024 on 3 May 2024.  These place climate change mitigation and adaptation reporting duties on large-sized specified public bodies. I am currently engaging with Executive colleagues on draft regulations that will set Northern Ireland’s first three carbon budgets and a 2040 emissions reduction target. Once Executive agreement is obtained I will bring the regulations forward for approval by the Assembly. In addition, DAERA is leading the development of Northern Ireland’s first Climate Action Plan. This plan will set out government’s collective approach to delivering the emissions reductions required to achieve Northern Ireland’s first carbon budget. Officials across all departments are in the process of finalising the first draft Climate Action Plan covering carbon budget 2023-2027 and I intend to bring this to the Executive soon. Work is also progressing around the establishment of a Just Transition Commission. I envisage that proposals around the constitution of the Just Transition Commission, including the draft Regulations that will establish the Commission, will be published for consultation in the last quarter of 2024. I welcome the commitment by the Executive, in the draft Programme for Government published this week, to deliver on each of these requirements and I look forward to engaging with Executive colleagues in progressing them.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the (i) methodology; and (ii) rate of uptake used by economists in his Department to calculate the initial estimated cost of implementing paid carers leave, as outlined in the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill consultation.
answer_text: The Good Jobs consultation covers a wide range of potential employment rights changes, including the introduction of a new Carer’s leave entitlement. The consultation asks respondents for their views whether this entitlement should be a paid or unpaid right. To inform respondents in making their response, an initial estimated cost of a paid entitlement of £60 million per annum was referenced.Census 2021 data was utilised to identify the number of unpaid carers in employment. This cohort was split by gender; age group and hours worked. The proportion of self-employed from the annual population survey was applied to the various groupings so as to estimate the numbers of unpaid carers who are employees within each group. This methodology identified the maximum number of potential beneficiaries based upon the most recently available data.The median weekly wage excluding overtime within each group, as indicated by the 2023 Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings (ASHE), was multiplied by the estimated number within each grouping. Full wage costs were used to identify the maximum amount potentially payable.  This does not indicate any policy position on the level that the entitlement, should it be a paid one, would be paid at. The full wage cost using this methodology was estimated to be c£54m per annum. Non-wage labour costs, including national insurance and pension contributions, have been estimated by ONS to equal 17.95% of wages. Applying this percentage to the estimated wage costs of £54m results in annual costs of £9.7m, giving a combined total of £63.8m.This approach has not taken into account any increase in population since the last census, nor the ageing population trends. In addition, it has not taken into account the intended beneficial impact of a paid entitlement to allow paid carers to increase their hours or enter/re-enter the workforce which may in turn escalate the level of demand. The figures above relate to absence costs only and do not take into account additional costs which may fall to employers or government (e.g. cost of regulation, familiarisation costs, administrative costs, etc). For the purpose of the consultation the estimated upper cost of a paid entitlement was rounded down to £60m.Further work is ongoing to refine these calculations together with cross departmental engagement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 13321/22-27, for an update on the work that has been undertaken to commission a review of the Northern Ireland Curriculum.
answer_text: My officials are currently progressing work on agreeing the Terms of Reference and will announce our proposed way forward later this month.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the progress that has been made on implementing the Developing a More Strategic Approach to 14 to 19 Education and Training - a Framework to Transform 14 to 19 Education and Training Provision.
answer_text: The 14-19 Framework is a joint endeavour between my Department and the Department of Education. The Minister for Education and I have met to discuss our approach to this work and our shared priorities, taking account of the current education and skills environment. As a first step, we have prioritised Careers as a priority area for delivery and tasked officials from both of our Departments to produce a joint programme of work. Post-school transition and provision for young people with Special Education Needs is also a priority area.  A team is now in place and work is underway to scope the existing provision with a view to providing options to improve both access and outcomes for our young people with Special Educational Needs.  Assessing the relevance and effectiveness of approaches, including legislative frameworks, in place in other jurisdictions will be an important aspect of this work.   This indicates our commitment to collaboration in the interests of our young people during this important phase of their lives. On the wider framework, the Minister for Education and I will be considering how best to prioritise the other elements, building on this collaborative approach and using insights from stakeholders on the ground.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 11244/22-27, for an update on the procurement of an Integrated Consultancy Team to undertake feasibility studies on potential sites for Mid Down Integrated College.
answer_text: My Department has been working with stakeholders to determine the scope of works for the appointment of the Integrated Consultant Team (ICT) for Mid Down Integrated College. On completion of this work, the procurement process will be initiated.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the operation of the Victims' Payments Board.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when the budget allocation to the Central Good Relations Fund 2024-25 will be confirmed.
answer_text: The budget for the Central Good Relations Fund 2024-25 was finalised on the 2 July 2024, with an allocation of £2.275m awarded.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, in light of the recently announced interim measures, to detail the timeline for bringing the long-term Early Learning and Childcare strategy to the Executive for approval.
answer_text: As stated in my Ministerial Statement on 23rd May, while it will be challenging, my aim is to have the childcare subsidy scheme operational for September.  My officials remain committed to working to this timescale.The Executive has agreed that the focus over the coming months should be on implementation of the early learning and childcare measures announced for 2024/25. Evaluation of these measures and analysis of the data due to be collected from parents and providers in 2024/25 will inform the development of the longer-term Strategy and a feasible timeline to deliver it.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what plans he has to address the disparities in pricing between Irish Rail and Translink Enterprise for train services between Belfast and Dublin.
